   232	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
   233	  const triggers_json = JSON.stringify(triggerPhrases);
   234	  const embedding_buffer = to_embedding_buffer(embedding);
   235	  const canonicalFilePath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
   236	
   237	  const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
   238	  const existing = appendOnly
   239	    ? null
   240	    : stmts.get_by_folder_and_path.get(specFolder, canonicalFilePath, filePath, anchorId, anchorId);
   241	
   242	  if (existing && !appendOnly) {
   243	    return update_memory({
   244	      id: existing.id,
   245	      title: title ?? undefined,
   246	      triggerPhrases,
   247	      importanceWeight,
   248	      embedding,
   249	      encodingIntent,
   250	      documentType,
   251	      specLevel,
   252	      contentText,
   253	      qualityScore,
   254	      qualityFlags,
   255	      canonicalFilePath,
   256	    }, database);
   257	  }
   258	
   259	  const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
   260	
   261	  const index_memory_tx = database.transaction(() => {
   262	    const embedding_status = sqlite_vec ? 'success' : 'pending';
   263	
   264	    const result = database.prepare(`
   265	      INSERT INTO memory_index (
   266	        spec_folder, file_path, canonical_file_path, anchor_id, title, trigger_phrases,
   267	        importance_weight, created_at, updated_at, embedding_model,
   268	        embedding_generated_at, embedding_status, encoding_intent, document_type, spec_level,
   269	        content_text, quality_score, quality_flags, parent_id
   270	      ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   271	    `).run(
   272	      specFolder, filePath, canonicalFilePath, anchorId, title, triggers_json,
   273	      importanceWeight, now, now, embeddingsProvider.getModelName(), now, embedding_status,
   274	      encodingIntent ?? 'document', documentType, specLevel, contentText, qualityScore, JSON.stringify(qualityFlags), parentId
   275	    );
   276	
   277	    const row_id = BigInt(result.lastInsertRowid);
   278	    const metadata_id = Number(row_id);
   279	
   280	    upsert_active_projection(database, specFolder, canonicalFilePath, anchorId, metadata_id, now);
   281	
   282	    if (sqlite_vec) {
   283	      // Remove orphaned vec_memories entry before insert
   284	      database.prepare(`DELETE FROM ${activeVectorSource('vec_memories')} WHERE rowid = ?`).run(row_id);
   285	      database.prepare(`
   286	        INSERT INTO ${activeVectorSource('vec_memories')} (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)
   287	      `).run(row_id, embedding_buffer);
   288	    }
   289	
   290	    refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(database, specFolder);
   291	    // H3 FIX: Invalidate search cache after insert
   292	    clear_search_cache();
   293	
   294	    return metadata_id;
   295	  });
   296	
   297	  return index_memory_tx();
   298	}
   299	
   300	// Deferred indexing - entry searchable via BM25/FTS5 only
   301	/**
   302	 * Indexes a memory record without storing an embedding yet.
   303	 * @param params - The deferred memory values to index.
   304	 * @returns The indexed memory identifier.
   305	 */
   306	export function index_memory_deferred(
   307	  params: IndexMemoryDeferredParams,
   308	  database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
   309	): number {
   310	  const {
   311	    specFolder,
   312	    filePath,
   313	    parentId = null,
   314	    anchorId = null,
   315	    title = null,
   520	      UPDATE memory_index SET ${updates.join(', ')} WHERE id = ?
   521	    `).run(...values);
   522	
   523	    // B11: Return early if the target row no longer exists.
   524	    if (updateResult.changes === 0) {
   525	      return id;
   526	    }
   527	
   528	    const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
   529	    if (embedding && sqlite_vec) {
   530	      const expected_dim = get_embedding_dim();
   531	      if (embedding.length !== expected_dim) {
   532	        console.warn(`[vector-index] Embedding dimension mismatch in update: expected ${expected_dim}, got ${embedding.length}`);
   533	        throw new VectorIndexError(
   534	          `Embedding must be ${expected_dim} dimensions, got ${embedding.length}`,
   535	          VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION,
   536	        );
   537	      }
   538	
   539	      const embedding_buffer = to_embedding_buffer(embedding);
   540	      database.prepare(`DELETE FROM ${activeVectorSource('vec_memories')} WHERE rowid = ?`).run(BigInt(id));
   541	      database.prepare(`
   542	        INSERT INTO ${activeVectorSource('vec_memories')} (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)
   543	      `).run(BigInt(id), embedding_buffer);
   544	      // H1 FIX: Mark success only after vector write confirmed
   545	      database.prepare('UPDATE memory_index SET embedding_status = ? WHERE id = ?').run('success', id);
   546	    }
   547	
   548	    if (existingRow?.spec_folder) {
   549	      const projectionPath = canonicalFilePath
   550	        ?? existingRow.canonical_file_path
   551	        ?? getCanonicalPathKey(existingRow.file_path ?? '');
   552	      upsert_active_projection(database, existingRow.spec_folder, projectionPath, existingRow.anchor_id ?? null, id, now);
   553	    }
   554	
   555	    if (existingRow?.spec_folder) {
   556	      refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(database, existingRow.spec_folder);
   557	    }
   558	    // H3 FIX: Invalidate search cache after update
   559	    clear_search_cache();
   560	
   561	    return id;
   562	  });
   563	
   564	  return update_memory_tx();
   565	}
   566	
   567	/**
   568	 * Deletes a memory and its related index records.
   569	 * @param id - The memory identifier.
   570	 * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
   571	 * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates mutation or store initialization failures from the delete pipeline.
   572	 * @example
   573	 * ```ts
   574	 * const deleted = delete_memory(42);
   575	 * ```
   576	 */
   577	export function delete_memory(id: number, database: Database.Database = initialize_db()): boolean {
   578	  return delete_memory_from_database(database, id);
   579	}

   581	/**
   582	 * Deletes a memory and related index records using the provided database handle.
   583	 * @param database - The database containing the target memory.
   584	 * @param id - The memory identifier.
   585	 * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
   720	    try {
   721	      const row = database.prepare('SELECT spec_folder FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(id) as { spec_folder?: string | null } | undefined;
   722	      specFolderById.set(id, row?.spec_folder ?? null);
   723	    } catch (_error: unknown) {
   724	      specFolderById.set(id, null);
   725	    }
   726	  }
   727	
   728	  const delete_transaction = database.transaction(() => {
   729	    let transactionDeleted = 0;
   730	    let transactionFailed = 0;
   731	    const failed_ids: number[] = [];
   732	
   733	    for (const id of memory_ids) {
   734	      try {
   735	        if (sqlite_vec) {
   736	          try {
   737	            database.prepare(`DELETE FROM ${activeVectorSource('vec_memories')} WHERE rowid = ?`).run(BigInt(id));
   738	          } catch (vec_error: unknown) {
   739	            console.warn(`[VectorIndex] Failed to delete vector for memory ${id}: ${get_error_message(vec_error)}`);
   740	          }
   741	        }
   742	
   743	        deleteAncillaryMemoryRows(database, id);
   744	        const result = database.prepare('DELETE FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').run(id);
   745	        if (result.changes > 0) {
   746	          // Memory_history rows are intentionally preserved after deletion
   747	          // So DELETE audit events recorded here persist as audit trail.
   748	          try {
   749	            recordHistory(id, 'DELETE', null, null, 'mcp:delete_memories', specFolderById.get(id) ?? null);
   750	          } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
   751	            // BEST-EFFORT HISTORY WRITES MUST NOT MASK A SUCCESSFUL DELETE.
   752	          }
   753	          transactionDeleted++;
   754	          deletedIds.push(id);
   755	        } else {
   756	          transactionFailed++;
   757	          failed_ids.push(id);
   758	        }
   759	      } catch (e: unknown) {
   760	        console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to delete memory ${id}: ${get_error_message(e)}`);

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:167:| `query-router.ts` | Routes hybrid retrieval; `shouldPreserveBm25` / `shouldPreserveGraph` overrides activate bm25/graph channels for intent-driven and entity-rich queries regardless of complexity tier. Reads `SPECKIT_GRAPH_CHANNEL_PRESERVATION` env flag. |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/README.md:175:`query-router.ts` can preserve graph and BM25 channels even when the complexity tier would otherwise skip them. The graph path is guarded by `SPECKIT_GRAPH_CHANNEL_PRESERVATION`, intent signals, and the cached entity-density score from `entity-density.ts`; entity-rich queries stay eligible for causal graph retrieval because they are more likely to benefit from high-fanout memory rows. `routing-telemetry.ts` records the resulting channel choices in a rolling 200-decision window so maintainers can inspect graph invocation rates through health telemetry. The full design lives in the local implementation notes.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:49:### Memory-DB `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50:Every memory write funnels through one `INSERT` into `memory_index`, which fires the FTS5 sync trigger `memory_fts_insert` (`INSERT INTO memory_fts(rowid,...) VALUES(new.id,...)`). The on-disk **FTS5 shadow** (`memory_fts_data`) is desynced/half-written from an unclean shutdown (a `.unclean-shutdown` marker is present), so the trigger's shadow insert hits a duplicate rowid → `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`, aborting the whole `memory_index` insert. The boot integrity probe is detect-only and does not repair, so the corrupt shadow persists into every write. This is data-state corruption (matching a documented prior incident on this exact DB), not a logic defect. **Fix (operator-gated): rebuild the FTS shadow** — `INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')` then `('integrity-check')`, then VACUUM — via `memory_health autoRepair` or the FTS runbook, on a DB copy first. Touches only the derived index, not source rows.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:69:The two issues were investigated read-only by parallel agents and cross-checked against source + the documented prior corruption bug-report. Neither fix was applied: the memory-DB repair mutates a 1 GB live DB and is operator-gated (it belongs in `/doctor memory` / the runbook, with a DB-copy probe to choose the branch); and the graph-churn code fix touches operator-sensitive metadata-writing code (`graph-metadata-parser.ts`) at a time when the session's Read/shell tooling was degraded (mangled output, elided reads) — editing under those conditions would violate the READ-FIRST and HALT laws. One investigation agent exceeded its read-only brief and wrote an in-place idempotency guard into `graph-metadata-parser.ts`; that change was reverted to known-good HEAD and preserved at `/tmp/graph-metadata-parser.AGENT-PROPOSED.ts` as a reference to re-derive (not trust blindly) when the fix is applied.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/20--remediation-revalidation/02-memory-health-auto-repair.md:3:description: "Documents the confirmed repair path behind `memory_health`, including FTS rebuilds, trigger-cache refreshes, and orphan cleanup for causal edges, vectors, and chunks."
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/20--remediation-revalidation/02-memory-health-auto-repair.md:10:Documents the confirmed repair path behind `memory_health`, including FTS rebuilds, trigger-cache refreshes, and orphan cleanup for causal edges, vectors, and chunks.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/20--remediation-revalidation/02-memory-health-auto-repair.md:12:This remediation flow turns `memory_health` from a read-only diagnostic into a guarded repair entry point. It does not silently mutate the database: callers must opt in with `autoRepair: true` and explicitly confirm the action before any repair step runs.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/20--remediation-revalidation/02-memory-health-auto-repair.md:18:`memory_health` exposes auto-repair only in full-report mode. If a caller sets `autoRepair: true` without `confirmed: true`, the handler returns a confirmation-required success envelope with the planned actions (`fts_rebuild`, `trigger_cache_refresh`, `orphan_edges_cleanup`, `orphan_vector_cleanup`) and performs no mutation.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/20--remediation-revalidation/02-memory-health-auto-repair.md:20:Once confirmed, the handler runs a staged repair sequence. First it compares row counts between `memory_index` and `memory_fts`; if the counts diverge, it issues the SQLite FTS rebuild command and immediately refreshes the trigger cache so lexical and trigger lookups stay aligned with the rebuilt index. Next it initializes the causal-edge store and removes orphaned causal edges that reference deleted memories.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/20--remediation-revalidation/01-category-stub.md:12:Operator remediation lives in `memory_health`, which can diagnose inconsistencies and, with explicit confirmation, perform bounded repair actions. Human-in-the-loop revalidation lives in `memory_validate`, which feeds confidence, promotion, negative feedback, and learned-feedback pipelines after results are used, while checkpoints provide the rollback safety net around destructive or high-risk remediation work. This means audit phase `021-remediation-revalidation` is now a real runtime category: the system both blocks bad writes and exposes targeted repair and revalidation paths after data has already been indexed.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/20--remediation-revalidation/01-category-stub.md:25:- `memory_health` is the operator-facing remediation tool. In `reportMode: "full"` it inspects database connectivity, vector search readiness, FTS consistency, alias divergence, and general integrity. With `autoRepair:true`, it never executes immediately; the caller must repeat the request with `confirmed:true`.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/20--remediation-revalidation/01-category-stub.md:26:- Confirmed `memory_health` auto-repair is intentionally bounded. It can rebuild `memory_fts`, refresh the trigger cache, clean orphaned causal edges, and auto-clean orphaned vectors and chunks. The response tracks `repair.requested`, `attempted`, `repaired`, `partialSuccess`, `actions`, `warnings`, and `errors`, so mixed outcomes stay visible instead of being collapsed into a single success flag.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/20--remediation-revalidation/01-category-stub.md:27:- `memory_health` also keeps a diagnostics-only lane. `reportMode: "divergent_aliases"` surfaces conflicting `.opencode/specs` versus `specs/` alias groups, but auto-repair is disabled there and the handler returns guidance instead of mutation.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/05--lifecycle/03-checkpoint-restore-checkpointrestore.md:12:This brings your knowledge base back to a previous snapshot, like using the undo button on a massive scale. If a replace-style restore fails partway through, nothing changes and your current data stays safe. In merge mode, successful rows can still commit while failures are reported as warnings. Restored memories are immediately searchable without any extra steps. The restore path now also enters a maintenance barrier that blocks mutation traffic until the restore lifecycle and post-restore rebuilds finish.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/05--lifecycle/03-checkpoint-restore-checkpointrestore.md:24:After restore, vectors are restored from the checkpoint snapshot when vector payloads are present. The restore handler then clears in-memory search/constitutional caches, rebuilds BM25 from live DB content when BM25 is enabled and refreshes the trigger cache. This keeps restored memories immediately discoverable without forcing a full re-embedding pass.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/05--lifecycle/03-checkpoint-restore-checkpointrestore.md:26:Checkpoint restore now acquires a module-level maintenance barrier before restore mutations begin and keeps it active through the post-restore rebuild sequence. While that barrier is active, concurrent `checkpoint_restore` calls and mutation traffic from `memory_save`, `memory_index_scan` and `memory_bulk_delete` fail fast with `E_RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS` instead of racing the restore lifecycle.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-types.ts:50:/** Arguments for the memory_health handler. */
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-types.ts:56:  autoRepair?: boolean;
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.3.md:11:#### Database restore could corrupt data if saves happened during rebuild
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.3.md:13:When you restore a checkpoint, the system performs a large restore transaction and then rebuilds several derived tables (search indexes, text indexes, graph caches). During that rebuild window -- which can take seconds -- nothing prevented normal operations like saving new memories, running index scans, or bulk deleting. If a save happened mid-rebuild, the newly saved data could reference structures that were about to be overwritten by the restore, leaving the database in an inconsistent state. The restore process now sets a maintenance barrier before it begins and clears it only after all rebuilds complete. Any mutation operation (save, index scan, bulk delete) that arrives while the barrier is up gets an immediate, clear rejection (`E_RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS`) instead of silently proceeding into a half-rebuilt database. If the restore fails or crashes, the barrier is released in the cleanup handler so the system doesn't get permanently locked.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.3.md:48:The system maintains two text search engines: SQLite's built-in FTS5 (fast, always consistent with the database) and an in-memory BM25 index (slower, requires full rebuild at startup, can drift out of sync). Both ran by default. The BM25 engine performed a synchronous full table scan at startup to build its index, blocking the server from accepting requests. BM25 is now opt-in behind an explicit `ENABLE_BM25` flag. FTS5 is the default and only text search engine unless you specifically enable BM25. When BM25 is enabled, its startup rebuild is now incremental (syncing only changed rows) and deferred to background warmup instead of blocking the main thread.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v3.0.1.3.md:123:When chunking a large file, the system updated the text search index (BM25) with the new parent summary immediately. If all chunks subsequently failed to index, the system correctly rolled back to the old parent -- but the text search index still had the new summary. The old parent's content was invisible to word-based searches until the next full rebuild. The text search index update is now delayed until at least one chunk succeeds. If all chunks fail and the system falls back to the old parent, the text search index was never modified, so it stays correct automatically.

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-1.out.txt:4240:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:49:### Memory-DB `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-1.out.txt:4241:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50:Every memory write funnels through one `INSERT` into `memory_index`, which fires the FTS5 sync trigger `memory_fts_insert` (`INSERT INTO memory_fts(rowid,...) VALUES(new.id,...)`). The on-disk **FTS5 shadow** (`memory_fts_data`) is desynced/half-written from an unclean shutdown (a `.unclean-shutdown` marker is present), so the trigger's shadow insert hits a duplicate rowid → `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`, aborting the whole `memory_index` insert. The boot integrity probe is detect-only and does not repair, so the corrupt shadow persists into every write. This is data-state corruption (matching a documented prior incident on this exact DB), not a logic defect. **Fix (operator-gated): rebuild the FTS shadow** — `INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')` then `('integrity-check')`, then VACUUM — via `memory_health autoRepair` or the FTS runbook, on a DB copy first. Touches only the derived index, not source rows.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-1.out.txt:4244:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:69:The two issues were investigated read-only by parallel agents and cross-checked against source + the documented prior corruption bug-report. Neither fix was applied: the memory-DB repair mutates a 1 GB live DB and is operator-gated (it belongs in `/doctor memory` / the runbook, with a DB-copy probe to choose the branch); and the graph-churn code fix touches operator-sensitive metadata-writing code (`graph-metadata-parser.ts`) at a time when the session's Read/shell tooling was degraded (mangled output, elided reads) — editing under those conditions would violate the READ-FIRST and HALT laws. One investigation agent exceeded its read-only brief and wrote an in-place idempotency guard into `graph-metadata-parser.ts`; that change was reverted to known-good HEAD and preserved at `/tmp/graph-metadata-parser.AGENT-PROPOSED.ts` as a reference to re-derive (not trust blindly) when the fix is applied.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-1.out.txt:4246:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:82:| Prefer FTS-rebuild over id-dedupe for the DB | Touches only the derived index; matches the documented prior fix; never `INSERT OR REPLACE` on `memory_index` (would cascade-delete FK children) |

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/05--lifecycle/01-checkpoint-creation-checkpointcreate.md:18:Named snapshots capture the current memory state by serializing all 18 tables listed in `CHECKPOINT_MANIFEST.snapshot` (`memory_index`, `vec_memories`, `vec_metadata`, `working_memory`, `causal_edges`, `weight_history`, `memory_history`, `mutation_ledger`, `memory_conflicts`, `memory_corrections`, `adaptive_signal_events`, `negative_feedback_events`, `learned_feedback_audit`, `session_learning`, `governance_audit`, `session_state`, `session_sent_memories`, `memory_summaries`) into a gzip-compressed JSON blob stored in the `checkpoints` table, not just a small core subset. The checkpoint metadata also records the 9 derived tables in `CHECKPOINT_MANIFEST.rebuild` (`memory_lineage`, `active_memory_projection`, `memory_entities`, `entity_catalog`, `degree_snapshots`, `community_assignments`, `graph_communities`, `community_summaries`, `memory_fts`), so restore knows which tables must be regenerated rather than restored verbatim. You can scope a snapshot to a specific spec folder if you only care about preserving one area of the system.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v2.3.0.14.md:1:This release bulk index hard-clean + z_archive indexing parity - Added z_archive spec-doc discovery support, hardened forced indexing behavior to avoid PE/reconsolidation collapse during rebuilds, and completed a clean full re-index with alias cleanup and database sync back to main paths. .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v2.3.0.14.md:24:There was a reliability problem around Force-path hardening. Force-path hardening: during force: true indexing, prediction-error candidate selection and reconsolidation-on-save are skipped to prevent duplicate suppression/merge behavior in bulk rebuild scenarios. This matters because it reduces confusing failures and makes the system more dependable.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/changelog/v2.3.0.14.md:84:- No schema migration required. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-2.out.txt:805:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/spec.md:60:Two live-infra issues degrade the spec-memory subsystem. (1) The spec-memory MCP throws `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY` on `memory_match_triggers`, `memory_index_scan`, and `memory_save`, blocking every memory write/index path — present since session start, caused by corrupted on-disk DB state after an unclean shutdown, not a logic defect. .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-2.out.txt:872:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:49:### Memory-DB `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-2.out.txt:873:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50:Every memory write funnels through one `INSERT` into `memory_index`, which fires the FTS5 sync trigger `memory_fts_insert` (`INSERT INTO memory_fts(rowid,...) VALUES(new.id,...)`). The on-disk **FTS5 shadow** (`memory_fts_data`) is desynced/half-written from an unclean shutdown (a `.unclean-shutdown` marker is present), so the trigger's shadow insert hits a duplicate rowid → `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`, aborting the whole `memory_index` insert. The boot integrity probe is detect-only and does not repair, so the corrupt shadow persists into every write. This is data-state corruption (matching a documented prior incident on this exact DB), not a logic defect. **Fix (operator-gated): rebuild the FTS shadow** — `INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')` then `('integrity-check')`, then VACUUM — via `memory_health autoRepair` or the FTS runbook, on a DB copy first. Touches only the derived index, not source rows.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-2.out.txt:874:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:79:| Repair the memory DB via FTS5 rebuild (DB-copy probe first) | quick_check ok + no duplicate ids ruled out Branch 2; the malformed FTS5 shadow (Branch 1) is fixed by `INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')`, which touches only the derived index, not the 30670 source rows. Proven on a copy, then applied live with a pre-fix backup. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-2.out.txt:875:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:82:| Prefer FTS-rebuild over id-dedupe for the DB | Touches only the derived index; matches the documented prior fix; never `INSERT OR REPLACE` on `memory_index` (would cascade-delete FK children) |

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-2.out.txt:1851:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-1.out.txt:4240:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:49:### Memory-DB `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-2.out.txt:1852:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-1.out.txt:4241:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50:Every memory write funnels through one `INSERT` into `memory_index`, which fires the FTS5 sync trigger `memory_fts_insert` (`INSERT INTO memory_fts(rowid,...) VALUES(new.id,...)`). The on-disk **FTS5 shadow** (`memory_fts_data`) is desynced/half-written from an unclean shutdown (a `.unclean-shutdown` marker is present), so the trigger's shadow insert hits a duplicate rowid → `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`, aborting the whole `memory_index` insert. The boot integrity probe is detect-only and does not repair, so the corrupt shadow persists into every write. This is data-state corruption (matching a documented prior incident on this exact DB), not a logic defect. **Fix (operator-gated): rebuild the FTS shadow** — `INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')` then `('integrity-check')`, then VACUUM — via `memory_health autoRepair` or the FTS runbook, on a DB copy first. Touches only the derived index, not source rows.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-2.out.txt:1853:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-1.out.txt:4246:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:82:| Prefer FTS-rebuild over id-dedupe for the DB | Touches only the derived index; matches the documented prior fix; never `INSERT OR REPLACE` on `memory_index` (would cascade-delete FK children) |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-2.out.txt:1855:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/prompts/iter-1.out.txt:4249:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:105:1. **Memory-DB: fixed at the SQLite layer, not yet exercised through MCP.** The FTS5 shadow rebuild is verified (integrity-check ok, writes succeed), but the mk-spec-memory daemon was down this session, so the fix has not been re-tested via `memory_save`/`memory_index_scan`/`memory_match_triggers`. The next daemon boot reopens a clean DB; if corruption recurs it points to the abrupt-kill trigger (see the durability cluster, packet 009) rather than this repair.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/14--pipeline-architecture/11-pipeline-and-mutation-hardening.md:12:Ten small but important baseline fixes were applied to make the system more robust. Some exposed missing options that were supposed to be available. Others fixed cleanup problems where deleting a spec-doc record left orphaned records behind. A few improved how the system handles word variations in searches. Later hardening passes added more guardrails on top of that baseline, including a checkpoint restore maintenance barrier that blocks mutation traffic while restore and rebuild work is in flight.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/14--pipeline-architecture/11-pipeline-and-mutation-hardening.md:43:- **Checkpoint restore maintenance barrier (T300):** `restoreCheckpoint()` now flips a module-level restore-in-progress flag before restore mutations begin and holds it through post-restore rebuilds. Concurrent `checkpoint_restore` calls plus mutation traffic from `memory_save`, `memory_index_scan` and `memory_bulk_delete` fail fast with `E_RESTORE_IN_PROGRESS` instead of racing restore-side rebuilds. The barrier is cleared in a `finally` path, so both successful and failed restores reopen mutation traffic.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/14--pipeline-architecture/22-mcp-server-public-api-barrel.md:26:The barrel also intentionally promotes several curated internal helpers. Folder-discovery helpers from `lib/search/folder-discovery.ts` include `generatePerFolderDescription`, `savePerFolderDescription`, `loadPerFolderDescription`, `extractKeywords`, `slugifyFolderName`, and the `PerFolderDescription` type. Entity extraction support comes from `lib/extraction/entity-extractor.ts` via `extractEntities` and `rebuildAutoEntities`. .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/03--discovery/03-health-diagnostics-memoryhealth.md:2:title: "Health diagnostics (memory_health)"
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/03--discovery/03-health-diagnostics-memoryhealth.md:6:# Health diagnostics (memory_health)
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/03--discovery/03-health-diagnostics-memoryhealth.md:18:`memory_health` has two report modes. `full` returns system diagnostics: database connectivity, embedding model readiness, vector-search availability, memory count, uptime, server version, alias-conflict summary, repair metadata, embedding provider details, and an `embeddingRetry` snapshot. `divergent_aliases` returns a compact triage payload that focuses only on alias groups whose `specs/` and `.opencode/specs/` variants have different content hashes, and it also includes the same top-level `embeddingRetry` field.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/03--discovery/03-health-diagnostics-memoryhealth.md:24:`autoRepair: true` without `confirmed: true` does not execute repair actions. Instead, the handler returns a confirmation-only success response describing the actions it would take. The public tool schema now accepts `confirmed`, so the documented confirmation flow is reachable through real MCP calls as well as direct handler tests.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/02--mutation/02-memory-metadata-update-memoryupdate.md:26:BM25 refresh is not limited to title edits. Whenever either `title` or `triggerPhrases` changes, the handler re-reads the updated row and rebuilds the BM25 document from `title`, `content_text`, `trigger_phrases`, and `file_path` so lexical search stays aligned with metadata edits. Infrastructure-level BM25 outages are downgraded to warnings, but data-path BM25 failures from an otherwise live index still abort the transaction and roll the update back.

The script targets the active Memory MCP database, resolving the profile-derived filename via `shared/embeddings/profile.ts` (provider, model, dimension, and dtype keyed). After DB rebuilds or imports, operators should preview or refresh ground-truth mappings with `scripts/evals/map-ground-truth-ids.ts` before treating a new ablation run as comparable to older history.

.opencode/commands/memory/manage.md:992:### memory_health: Full Parameters
.opencode/commands/memory/manage.md:999:| `autoRepair` | boolean | false | Attempt best-effort repair actions (e.g., FTS rebuild) |
.opencode/commands/memory/manage.md:1000:| `confirmed` | boolean | false | Required with `autoRepair:true` to execute repairs. Without it, returns confirmation-only response |
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/29-setup-native-module-health-and-mcp-installation.md:3:description: "Spec-folder prerequisite validation, native module diagnostics and rebuild, Node ABI marker recording, and Spec Kit Memory MCP bootstrap/configuration scripts."
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/29-setup-native-module-health-and-mcp-installation.md:12:Taken together, these scripts validate the active feature-folder shape, install and build the Spec Kit workspace, probe native Node module health, rebuild ABI-sensitive packages after runtime changes, record a compatibility marker, and register the MCP server in `opencode.json`.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/29-setup-native-module-health-and-mcp-installation.md:22:5. Native module health is treated as part of installation, not a separate manual step. `install.sh` shells into `check-native-modules.sh`, prints the probe output verbatim, and if any `[FAIL]` marker appears it automatically runs `rebuild-native-modules.sh` with `n` piped into the optional Hugging Face cache prompt, then re-runs the health probe and aborts if failures remain.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/29-setup-native-module-health-and-mcp-installation.md:26:9. `rebuild-native-modules.sh` is the repair path for ABI drift. It rebuilds `better-sqlite3` inside `mcp_server`, runs `npm rebuild` in `shared/` when that workspace is installed, optionally clears the global Hugging Face cache interactively, and then calls `record-node-version.js` to rewrite `.node-version-marker` with the current Node version, module ABI, platform, architecture, and timestamp.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/29-setup-native-module-health-and-mcp-installation.md:28:For canonical post-rebuild verification, see the four reference docs under packet `008-mcp-daemon-rebuild-protocol/references/`: `mcp-rebuild-restart-protocol.md` documents the rebuild + restart sequence, `live-probe-template.md` defines the live-probe envelope used to confirm a fresh daemon is responding to MCP traffic, `dist-marker-grep-cheatsheet.md` lists the grep patterns for verifying a `dist/` artifact carries the expected source change, and `implementation-verification-checklist.md` is the canonical checklist run after every MCP rebuild.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/29-setup-native-module-health-and-mcp-installation.md:39:| `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/setup/rebuild-native-modules.sh` | Repair script | Rebuilds ABI-sensitive modules, optionally clears cache, and refreshes the Node-version marker |

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/31-evaluation-benchmark-and-import-policy-tooling.md:24:`map-ground-truth-ids.ts` is the read-only ground-truth provenance helper. It opens the active profile-derived production Memory MCP database in read-only mode, scores candidate parent memories for each non-negative query, writes a preview artifact to `/tmp/ground-truth-id-mapping.json`, and gives operators a concrete way to confirm whether `ground-truth.json` still matches the live parent-memory ID space after DB rebuilds or imports.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/12--query-intelligence/12-graph-channel-preservation.md:23:The entity-density cache is module-scoped in `entity-density.ts`. It rebuilds lazily on a 60s TTL or via `invalidateEntityDensityCache()`. Cold-start (no DB or empty `causal_edges` table) returns score 0, so the override stays inactive when the signal would be unreliable (REQ-006). Cache lookup is O(1) Set membership; the SQL build query (`INNER JOIN ... HAVING COUNT(*) >= 3`) runs only on refresh, not per-query.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/12--query-intelligence/12-graph-channel-preservation.md:25:A 200-decision rolling ring in `routing-telemetry.ts` records every routing decision via `recordInvocation(channels)`. `getSnapshot()` exposes per-channel counts, per-channel rates, and the headline `graphChannelInvocationRate`, which `memory_health.data.routing` surfaces alongside `channelInvocationRates`, `totalRecorded`, and `windowSize`.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/embedder_architecture.md:152:`memory_health` accepts `includeFullReport:true` for byte-aware runtime diagnostics. The extended report includes RSS, V8 heap totals, external memory, ArrayBuffer memory, V8 malloc counters, cache byte estimates for tool cache, trigger matcher regex retention, and the in-process embedding LRU.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/embedder_architecture.md:160:Operators can disable bridge mode with `SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED=1`, which restores legacy exit-on-lease-held behavior. `SPECKIT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS` caps concurrent bridge clients (default 8). `SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR` relocates the bridge socket directory and is **required on macOS** for all three launcher services: the default `<service-db>/daemon-ipc.sock` path is longer than the platform's 104-char `sun_path` limit, so `listen()` fails with `EINVAL` and the MCP handshake never completes. The committed runtime configs pin each service to `/tmp/mk-spec-memory`, `/tmp/mk-skill-advisor`, and `/tmp/mk-code-index`. Full `memory_health({ includeFullReport: true })` output includes `ipc_bridge.socket_path`, `secondary_clients_count`, and cumulative secondary message counters.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/embedder_architecture.md:166:`memory_health` is intentionally lightweight before the guard fires. It uses `tryGetDb()` and reports `runtime_initialized: false` without opening SQLite or spawning embedder sidecars. After the first guarded memory call, the same field reports `true`, and full reports include DB-backed cache and consistency data again.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/embedder_architecture.md:196:Full `memory_health` reports expose `sidecar_workers`, keyed by provider/model, with the worker pid, model, last request timestamp, idle age, and request count. An empty object means no sidecar is currently spawned.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/memory/embedder_architecture.md:204:Full `memory_health` reports expose `cache_byte_estimates.embedding_cache_by_profile`, with document/query breakdowns per profile. Use that field to verify profile switches do not stack unbounded historical cache rows.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/42-mcp-daemon-rebuild-restart-live-probe.md:20:The protocol is documented in four reference files under packet `008-mcp-daemon-rebuild-protocol/references/` and exercised by playbook scenario 278.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/42-mcp-daemon-rebuild-restart-live-probe.md:22:`mcp-rebuild-restart-protocol.md` documents the rebuild plus restart sequence and the per-client restart commands. OpenCode reloads tools, Claude Code restarts the binary, and the Codex and Gemini CLIs restart their binaries. The restart command differs per client because the MCP daemon process is owned by the host runtime, not the Spec Kit workspace.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/42-mcp-daemon-rebuild-restart-live-probe.md:28:`implementation-verification-checklist.md` is the canonical checklist run after every MCP rebuild. It chains the four parts in order and fails closed if any step is skipped. The checklist is the operator-facing surface; the other three references are the building blocks.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/16--tooling-and-scripts/45-embedder-set-dry-run-and-validation.md:14:A real embedder swap rebuilds the corpus vector table and re-embeds every indexed document, which can take 15 minutes or more on a populated workspace. The dry-run path lets operators preview the planned table name, dimension count, and reindex action without paying that cost, and the validation path turns typos into structured errors instead of partial swaps.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/07-smarter-memory-content-generation.md:18:Raw markdown including code fences, nested lists and YAML frontmatter was being embedded as-is, diluting embedding quality with formatting noise. A content normalizer now strips this noise before embedding generation and BM25 rebuild/index paths that call `normalizeContentForBM25()`.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/13--memory-quality-and-indexing/07-smarter-memory-content-generation.md:22:The normalizer has no feature flag because it is a non-destructive improvement. It is always active in the `memory-save.ts` embedding path and in BM25 rebuild/tokenization paths that call `normalizeContentForBM25()`. .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/references/config/hook_system.md:117:Claude Code uses native `UserPromptSubmit`, `SessionStart`, `PreCompact`, and `Stop` hooks. Gemini CLI uses native `BeforeAgent`, `SessionStart`, `PreCompress`, and `SessionEnd` hooks. Copilot CLI uses Copilot-supported writer scripts that refresh the Spec Kit managed block in `$HOME/.copilot/copilot-instructions.md`; hook output remains `{}` with NEXT-PROMPT freshness semantics, so the current prompt sees the PRIOR turn's brief, and the registration contract is documented in `mcp_server/hooks/copilot/README.md`. Do not use the stale merged `.claude/settings.local.json` wrapper shape for Copilot. OpenCode uses plugin-based transport rather than shell wrappers: `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-skill-advisor.js` delivers prompt-time advisor briefs through `experimental.chat.system.transform`, while `.opencode/plugins/spec-kit-compact-code-graph.js` and plugin `event` handlers cover startup, compaction, readiness, and session cleanup. Codex CLI only reports live native-hook readiness when `[features].codex_hooks = true` is enabled in `~/.codex/config.toml` or equivalent launch flags and user/workspace `hooks.json` is wired; on `UserPromptSubmit` timeout, Codex returns a stale fallback marker (`stale:true`, `reason:"timeout-fallback"`) and logs a structured warning instead of silently serving cold-start context. Use `/spec_kit:resume` when hooks are unavailable or disabled. If automatic hook delivery is unavailable in any runtime, or the advisor hook path is intentionally disabled (`SPECKIT_SKILL_ADVISOR_HOOK_DISABLED=1`), fall back to the canonical operator path: start with `/spec_kit:resume`, rebuild packet continuity from `handover.md -> _memory.continuity -> spec docs`, then use `session_bootstrap()` or `session_resume()` only when you need lower-level structural health or merged recovery detail.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:322:Before embedding generation, content normalization strips structural markdown noise. Seven primitives (frontmatter, anchors, HTML comments, code fences, tables, lists, headings) run in sequence to produce cleaner text for the embedding model. BM25 has a separate normalization entry point (`normalizeContentForBM25`) that currently delegates to the embedding normalizer, and it is used on rebuild-from-database paths. In live save paths, raw content is passed to BM25 tokenization (`addDocument`) before tokenizer normalization.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:582:### Health diagnostics (memory_health)
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:590:`memory_health` has two report modes. `full` returns system diagnostics: database connectivity, embedding model readiness, vector-search availability, memory count, uptime, server version, alias-conflict summary, repair metadata and embedding provider details. `divergent_aliases` returns a compact triage payload that focuses only on alias groups whose `` and `.opencode/specs/` variants have different content hashes.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:594:`autoRepair: true` without `confirmed: true` does not execute repair actions. Instead, the handler returns a confirmation-only success response describing the actions it would take. The public tool schema now accepts `confirmed`, so the documented confirmation flow is reachable through real MCP calls as well as direct handler tests.
.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/feature_catalog/feature_catalog.md:708:After restore, vectors are restored from the checkpoint snapshot when vector payloads are present. The restore handler then clears in-memory search/constitutional caches, rebuilds BM25 from live DB content when BM25 is enabled and refreshes the trigger cache. This keeps restored memories immediately discoverable without forcing a full re-embedding pass.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/README.md:548:The **memory system** is the librarian. When a session ends, `generate-context.js` updates the packet's canonical continuity surfaces so the next session can recover from packet-local sources first. The MCP server indexes those packet docs into vector, FTS5, and BM25 surfaces, while graph and degree signals are computed at retrieval time. When a new session starts, `/speckit:resume` rebuilds context from `handover.md`, `_memory.continuity`, and the packet docs. If you need deeper retrieval after that, `session_bootstrap()` bundles resume context, health, and structural readiness into one follow-up recovery call before broader `memory_context` work begins.

.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/benchmarks/benchmark-2026-05-21-fp16-rerank/benchmark_report.md:151:# 5) Pin spec-memory to Qwen + rebuild

codex
The marker semantics are more specific than the initial packet wording suggested: the DB layer writes `.unclean-shutdown` on every non-memory open, and only `close_db()` removes it after checkpoint plus `db.close()`. That makes any server path that skips `close_db()` look “unclean” even if the database is healthy at rest.

   337	// `getDetectedRuntime` was previously exported from this module
   338	// but had zero live consumers (only its own definition + the generated
   339	// `dist/context-server.d.ts` declaration). The variable still drives the
   340	// startup banner inside main(), so it stays as a module-private. Removing
   341	// the dead export keeps the public surface honest and unblocks future
   342	// refactors that touch the runtime detection path.

   345	
   346	function getUncleanShutdownMarkerPath(): string {
   347	  // Derive the marker dir from the ACTUAL opened DB path (vectorIndex.getDbPath()) — the same path the
   348	  // marker writer in vector-index-store uses — not the independently-derived DATABASE_PATH. Under a
   349	  // MEMORY_DB_PATH whose dirname differs from the resolved DB dir, DATABASE_PATH would point the boot check
   350	  // at the wrong dir and silently disable corruption detection. Falls back to
   351	  // DATABASE_PATH if the DB is not open yet (the boot check only runs post-connect, so getDbPath is set).
   352	  const dbPath = vectorIndex.getDbPath() || DATABASE_PATH;
   353	  return path.join(path.dirname(dbPath), UNCLEAN_SHUTDOWN_MARKER);
   354	}
   355	
   356	function getBootFtsIntegrityHealth(): BootFtsIntegrityHealth {
   357	  return bootFtsIntegrityHealth;
   358	}
   359	
   360	function runBootFtsIntegrityCheck(): void {
   361	  try {
   362	    vectorIndex.getDb()
   363	      .prepare("INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('integrity-check')")
   364	      .run();
   365	    bootFtsIntegrityHealth = 'ok';
   366	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   367	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   368	    bootFtsIntegrityHealth = 'corrupt';
   369	    console.error([
   370	      '[context-server] ===== FTS5 SHADOW INDEX CORRUPTION DETECTED =====',
   371	      '[context-server] Boot marker indicates the previous mk-spec-memory shutdown was unclean.',
   372	      `[context-server] FTS5 integrity-check failed: ${message}`,
   373	      '[context-server] DETECT-ONLY: no DB recovery, index reconstruction, swap, or deletion was attempted.',
   374	      `[context-server] Runbook: ${FTS_CORRUPTION_RUNBOOK}`,
   375	      '[context-server] =================================================',
   376	    ].join('\n'));
   377	  }
   378	}
   379	
   380	function scheduleBootFtsIntegrityCheck(): void {
   381	  if (!fs.existsSync(getUncleanShutdownMarkerPath())) {
   382	    bootFtsIntegrityHealth = 'skipped';
   383	    return;
   384	  }
   385	
   386	  registerTimeout(() => runBootFtsIntegrityCheck(), 0, { unref: true });
   387	}

   390	  task: string,
  1450	   7. GRACEFUL SHUTDOWN
  1451	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
  1452	
  1453	let shuttingDown = false;
  1454	// Hoist transport to module scope so shutdown handlers can close it
  1455	let transport: StdioServerTransport | null = null;
  1456	let transportConnectedAt: string | null = null;
  1457	let fileWatcher: FSWatcher | null = null;
  1458	let ipcBridge: IpcSocketServerHandle | null = null;
  1459	let launcherIdleMonitor: LauncherIdleMonitor | null = null;
  1460	
  1461	/** Maximum time (ms) to wait for async cleanup before force-exiting. */
  1462	const SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS = 5000;
  1463	
  1464	export const __testables = {
  1465	  runCleanupStep,
  1466	  runAsyncCleanupStep,
  1467	  main: () => main(),
  1468	  getBootFtsIntegrityHealth,
  1469	  normalizeGraphFilePath,
  1470	  extractFilePathsFromToolArgs,

  1472	
  1473	async function fatalShutdown(reason: string, exitCode: number): Promise<void> {
  1474	  if (shuttingDown) return;
  1475	  shuttingDown = true;
  1476	  console.error(`[context-server] ${reason}`);
  1477	
  1478	  runCleanupStep('sessionManager', () => sessionManager.shutdown());
  1479	  runCleanupStep('retryManager', () => retryManager.stopBackgroundJob());
  1480	  runCleanupStep('shadowEvaluationRuntime', () => shadowEvaluationRuntime.stopShadowEvaluationScheduler());
  1481	  runCleanupStep('scheduledGraphRefresh', () => cancelScheduledRefresh());
  1482	  runCleanupStep('accessTracker', () => accessTracker.reset());
  1483	  runCleanupStep('toolCache', () => toolCache.shutdown());
  1484	  runCleanupStep('launcherIdleMonitor', () => {
  1485	    if (launcherIdleMonitor) {
  1486	      launcherIdleMonitor.stop();
  1487	      launcherIdleMonitor = null;
  1488	    }
  1489	  });
  1490	
  1491	  let deadlineTimer: NodeJS.Timeout | undefined;
  1492	  const cleanup = (async () => {
  1493	    // Drain the file watcher FIRST: fileWatcher.close() awaits in-flight reindex/remove tasks,
  1494	    // which write through requireDb()/getDb() — and getDb() REOPENS the connection if it was
  1495	    // already closed. So closeDb() must run AFTER the drain, otherwise a draining task reopens
  1496	    // the DB and writes fresh WAL frames after the TRUNCATE checkpoint, leaving a non-empty WAL
  1497	    // at rest and defeating the durability guarantee. closeDb() is
  1498	    // synchronous, so it still runs to completion uninterrupted once reached; the SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE
  1499	    // race only bounds the awaited drains before it, and 010's autocheckpoint keeps the at-rest WAL
  1500	    // small even if a pathologically slow drain starves this step.
  1501	    await runAsyncCleanupStep('fileWatcher', async () => {
  1502	      if (fileWatcher) {
  1503	        await fileWatcher.close();
  1504	        fileWatcher = null;
  1505	      }
  1506	    });
  1507	    runCleanupStep('vectorIndex', () => vectorIndex.closeDb());
  1508	    // Close MCP transport on shutdown
  1509	    runCleanupStep('transport', () => {
  1510	      if (transport) {
  1511	        transport.close();
  1512	        transport = null;
  1513	        transportConnectedAt = null;
  1514	      }
  1515	    });
  1516	    await runAsyncCleanupStep('ipcBridge', async () => {
  1517	      if (ipcBridge) {
  1518	        await ipcBridge.close();
  1519	        ipcBridge = null;
  1520	      }
  1521	    });
  1522	    await runAsyncCleanupStep('shutdownHooks', async () => {
  1523	      await runShutdownHooks();
  1524	    });
  1525	    runCleanupStep('registeredTimers', () => clearAllTimers());
  1526	  })();
  1527	
  1528	  const timedOut = await Promise.race([
  1529	    cleanup.then(() => false),
  1530	    new Promise<boolean>((resolve) => {
  1531	      deadlineTimer = registerTimeout(() => resolve(true), SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS, { unref: true });
  1532	    }),
  1533	  ]);
  1534	
  1535	  if (deadlineTimer) {
  1536	    clearRegisteredTimer(deadlineTimer);
  1537	  }
  1538	
  1539	  if (timedOut) {
  1540	    console.error(`[context-server] Shutdown deadline exceeded after ${SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_MS}ms`);
  1541	  }
  1542	
  1543	  process.exit(exitCode);
  1544	}
  1545	
  1546	/** Remove indexed rows for watcher delete and rename events. */
  1547	async function removeIndexedMemoriesForFile(filePath: string): Promise<void> {
  1548	  const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
  1549	  if (!database) {
  1550	    return;
  1551	  }
  1552	
  1553	  const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
  1554	  let rows: Array<{ id: number; spec_folder: string | null }> = [];
  1555	
  1556	  try {
  1557	    rows = database.prepare(`
  1558	      SELECT id, spec_folder
  1559	      FROM memory_index
  1560	      WHERE canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?
  1561	      ORDER BY id ASC
  1562	    `).all(canonicalPath, filePath) as Array<{ id: number; spec_folder: string | null }>;
  1563	  } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1564	    rows = database.prepare(`
  1565	      SELECT id, spec_folder
  1566	      FROM memory_index
  1567	      WHERE file_path = ?
  1568	      ORDER BY id ASC
  1569	    `).all(filePath) as Array<{ id: number; spec_folder: string | null }>;
  1570	  }
  1571	
  1572	  let deletedCount = 0;
  1573	  for (const row of rows) {
  1574	    if (typeof row.id === 'number') {
  1575	      if (vectorIndex.deleteMemory(row.id)) {
  1576	        deletedCount += 1;
  1577	        // Record DELETE history only after confirmed deletion.
  1578	        try {
  1579	          recordHistory(row.id, 'DELETE', filePath ?? null, null, 'mcp:file_watcher', row.spec_folder ?? null);
  1580	        } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
  1581	          // History recording is best-effort in file-watcher path
  1582	        }
  1583	      }
  1584	    }
  1585	  }
  1586	
  1587	  if (deletedCount > 0) {
  1588	    try {
  1589	      runPostMutationHooks('delete', { filePath, deletedCount });
  1590	    } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1591	      // Non-throwing by design: file-watcher path must not crash the server.
  1592	    }
  1593	  }
  1594	}
  1595

  2047	    getActiveClientCount: () => getIpcBridgeStats().secondary_clients_count,
  2048	    onIdle: () => fatalShutdown('Launcher idle timeout reached, shutting down...', 0),
  2049	    log: (message) => console.error(message),
  2050	  });
  2051	  ipcBridge = await startIpcSocketServer({
  2052	    socketPath: resolveIpcSocketPath(DATABASE_DIR),
  2053	    createServer: () => {
  2054	      const secondaryServer = createContextMcpServer();
  2055	      registerContextServerHandlers(secondaryServer);
  2056	      return secondaryServer;
  2057	    },
  2058	    log: (message) => console.error(message),
  2059	    onActivity: () => launcherIdleMonitor?.markActivity(),
  2060	  });
  2061	  const rssMb = Math.round(process.memoryUsage().rss / 1024 / 1024);
  2062	  console.error(`[health] startup pid=${process.pid} rss=${rssMb}MB uptime=0s`);
  2063	  console.error('[context-server] Context MCP server running on stdio');
  2064	
  2065	}

  2067	const isMain = process.argv[1] && decodeURIComponent(import.meta.url).endsWith(process.argv[1].replace(/\\/g, '/'));
  2068	
  2069	if (isMain) {
  2070	  main().catch((err: unknown) => { console.error('[context-server] Fatal error:', err); process.exit(1); });
  2071	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: Runtime Shutdown Hooks
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	
     5	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     6	// 1. TYPE DEFINITIONS
     7	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     8	
     9	export type ShutdownHook = () => void | Promise<void>;
    10	
    11	interface ShutdownHookRecord {
    12	  readonly id: number;
    13	  readonly hook: ShutdownHook;
    14	  readonly timeoutMs: number;
    15	}
    16	
    17	export interface ShutdownHookResult {
    18	  readonly id: number;
    19	  readonly ok: boolean;
    20	  readonly error?: string;
    21	  readonly timedOut?: boolean;
    22	}
    23	
    24	interface RegisterShutdownHookOptions {
    25	  readonly timeoutMs?: number;
    26	}
    27	
    28	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    29	// 2. STATE
    30	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    31	
    32	const DEFAULT_HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS = 1_000;
    33	const hooks = new Map<number, ShutdownHookRecord>();
    34	let nextHookId = 1;
    35	let installed = false;
    36	let running = false;
    37	
    38	type ShutdownSignal = 'SIGINT' | 'SIGTERM';
    39	type ExitProcess = (code?: number) => never;
    40	
    41	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    42	// 3. CORE LOGIC
    43	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44	
    45	export function registerShutdownHook(
    46	  hook: ShutdownHook,
    47	  options: RegisterShutdownHookOptions = {},
    48	): () => void {
    49	  installProcessHooks();
    50	  const id = nextHookId++;
    51	  hooks.set(id, {
    52	    id,
    53	    hook,
    54	    timeoutMs: normalizeTimeout(options.timeoutMs),
    55	  });
    56	
    57	  return () => {
    58	    hooks.delete(id);
    59	  };
    60	}
    61	
    62	export async function runShutdownHooks(): Promise<ShutdownHookResult[]> {
    63	  if (running) return [];
    64	  running = true;
    65	  const results: ShutdownHookResult[] = [];
    66	
    67	  try {
    68	    for (const record of hooks.values()) {
    69	      results.push(await runOneHook(record));
    70	    }
    71	  } finally {
    72	    hooks.clear();
    73	    running = false;
    74	  }
    75	
    76	  return results;
    77	}
    78	
    79	export function getShutdownHookCount(): number {
    80	  return hooks.size;
    81	}
    82	
    83	export function clearShutdownHooksForTests(): void {
    84	  hooks.clear();
    85	  running = false;
    86	}
    87	
    88	export async function handleShutdownSignalForTests(
    89	  signal: ShutdownSignal,
    90	  exitProcess: ExitProcess,
    91	): Promise<void> {
    92	  await handleShutdownSignal(signal, exitProcess);
    93	}
    94	
    95	async function runOneHook(record: ShutdownHookRecord): Promise<ShutdownHookResult> {
    96	  let timer: NodeJS.Timeout | undefined;
    97	  try {
    98	    await Promise.race([
    99	      Promise.resolve().then(() => record.hook()),
   100	      new Promise<never>((_, reject) => {
   101	        timer = setTimeout(() => {
   102	          reject(Object.assign(new Error(`Shutdown hook timed out after ${record.timeoutMs}ms`), { timedOut: true }));
   103	        }, record.timeoutMs);
   104	        timer.unref?.();
   105	      }),
   106	    ]);
   107	    return { id: record.id, ok: true };
   108	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   109	    return {
   110	      id: record.id,
   111	      ok: false,
   112	      error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
   113	      timedOut: Boolean(error && typeof error === 'object' && 'timedOut' in error),
   114	    };
   115	  } finally {
   116	    if (timer) clearTimeout(timer);
   117	  }
   118	}
   119	
   120	function normalizeTimeout(timeoutMs: number | undefined): number {
   121	  if (timeoutMs === undefined) return DEFAULT_HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS;
   122	  return Number.isFinite(timeoutMs) && timeoutMs > 0 ? Math.floor(timeoutMs) : DEFAULT_HOOK_TIMEOUT_MS;
   123	}
   124	
   125	function installProcessHooks(): void {
   126	  if (installed) return;
   127	  installed = true;
   128	
   129	  process.once('SIGTERM', () => {
   130	    void handleShutdownSignal('SIGTERM', process.exit);
   131	  });
   132	  process.once('SIGINT', () => {
   133	    void handleShutdownSignal('SIGINT', process.exit);
   134	  });
   135	  process.once('beforeExit', () => {
   136	    void runShutdownHooks();
   137	  });
   138	}
   139	
   140	async function handleShutdownSignal(signal: ShutdownSignal, exitProcess: ExitProcess): Promise<void> {
   141	  const results = await runShutdownHooks();
   142	  const hookFailed = results.some((result) => !result.ok);
   143	  if (hookFailed) {
   144	    exitProcess(1);
   145	    return;
   146	  }
   147	  exitProcess(signal === 'SIGINT' ? 130 : 143);
   148	}

   623	  if (autoRepair && !confirmed) {
   624	    return createMCPSuccessResponse({
   625	      tool: 'memory_health',
   626	      summary: 'Confirmation required before auto-repair actions are executed',
   627	      data: {
   628	        status,
   629	        reportMode,
   630	        runtime_initialized: runtimeInitialized,
   631	        autoRepairRequested: true,
   632	        needsConfirmation: true,
   633	        actions: repairActions,
   634	        process: processHealth,
   635	        embeddingRetry,
   636	        ...(fullMemoryReport ?? {}),
   637	      },
   638	      hints: [
   639	        'Re-run memory_health with autoRepair:true and confirmed:true to execute repair actions.',
   640	      ],
   641	      startTime,
   642	    });
   643	  }
   644	
   645	  if (!database) {
   646	    hints.push('Database runtime has not initialized yet; the first memory-owning tool call will initialize it.');
   647	  }
   648	  if (!vectorIndex.isVectorSearchAvailable()) {
   649	    hints.push('Vector search unavailable - fallback to BM25');
   650	  }
   651	  // FTS5 consistency check
   652	  if (database) {
   653	    try {
   654	      const memoryCountRow = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM memory_index').get() as { count: number };
   655	      const ftsCountRow = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM memory_fts').get() as { count: number };
   656	      if (memoryCountRow.count !== ftsCountRow.count) {
   657	        hints.push(
   658	          `FTS5 index out of sync: memory_index has ${memoryCountRow.count} rows, memory_fts has ${ftsCountRow.count} rows. ` +
   659	          `Run memory_index_scan with force:true to rebuild FTS5 index.`
   660	        );
   661	
   662	        if (autoRepair) {
   663	          try {
   664	            database.exec("INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')");
   665	            repair.actions.push('fts_rebuild');
   666	
   667	            triggerMatcher.refreshTriggerCache();
   668	            repair.actions.push('trigger_cache_refresh');
   669	
   670	            const repairedFtsCountRow = database.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM memory_fts').get() as { count: number };
   671	            if (memoryCountRow.count === repairedFtsCountRow.count) {
   672	              trackRepairOutcome(true);
   673	              repair.actions.push('fts_consistency_verified');
   674	              hints.push('Auto-repair completed: FTS5 index rebuilt and trigger cache refreshed.');
   675	            } else {
   676	              trackRepairOutcome(false);
   677	              const warning = `Post-repair mismatch persists: memory_index=${memoryCountRow.count}, memory_fts=${repairedFtsCountRow.count}`;
   678	              repair.warnings.push(warning);
   679	              hints.push(`Auto-repair attempted, but mismatch remains (${warning}).`);
   680	            }
   681	          } catch (repairError: unknown) {
   682	            const message = toErrorMessage(repairError);
   683	            trackRepairOutcome(false);
   684	            repair.errors.push(sanitizeErrorForHint(message));
   685	            hints.push(`Auto-repair failed: ${sanitizeErrorForHint(message)}`);
   686	          }
   687	        }
   688	      }
   689	    } catch (e: unknown) {
   690	      const message = toErrorMessage(e);
   691	      hints.push(`FTS5 consistency check failed: ${sanitizeErrorForHint(message)}`);
   692	      if (autoRepair) {
   693	        trackRepairOutcome(false);
   694	        repair.errors.push(`Consistency check failed before repair: ${sanitizeErrorForHint(message)}`);
   695	      }
   696	    }
   697	  }
   698	  // cleanupOrphanedEdges was exported but
   699	  // Never invoked at runtime. Wire it into autoRepair so orphaned causal edges
   700	  // (referencing deleted memories) are cleaned up during health checks.
   701	  if (autoRepair && database) {
   702	    try {
   703	      // Fix F8 — ensure causal-edges DB init before orphan cleanup.
   704	      causalEdges.init(database);
   705	      const orphanResult = causalEdges.cleanupOrphanedEdges();
   706	      if (orphanResult.deleted > 0) {
   707	        trackRepairOutcome(true);
   708	        repair.actions.push(`orphan_edges_cleaned:${orphanResult.deleted}`);
   709	        hints.push(`Auto-repair: removed ${orphanResult.deleted} orphaned causal edge(s)`);
   710	      }
   711	    } catch (orphanError: unknown) {
   712	      trackRepairOutcome(false);
   713	      repair.errors.push(`Orphan edge cleanup failed: ${sanitizeErrorForHint(toErrorMessage(orphanError))}`);
   714	    }
   715	  }
   716	
   717	  if (autoRepair && database) {
   718	    try {
   719	      const integrityReport = vectorIndex.verifyIntegrity({ autoClean: true, cleanFiles });
   720	      const cleanedVectors = integrityReport.cleaned?.vectors ?? 0;

exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md | sed -n '45,112p'" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
    45	## What Was Built
    46	
    47	Two live-infra issues were root-caused. The memory-DB write failures are **resolved and MCP-verified** this session (clean daemon restart; the prior FTS5 rebuild held — `memory_health` reports HEALTHY with 0 constraint errors); the graph-churn code fix remains deferred (documented below).
    48	
    49	### Memory-DB `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`
    50	Every memory write funnels through one `INSERT` into `memory_index`, which fires the FTS5 sync trigger `memory_fts_insert` (`INSERT INTO memory_fts(rowid,...) VALUES(new.id,...)`). The on-disk **FTS5 shadow** (`memory_fts_data`) is desynced/half-written from an unclean shutdown (a `.unclean-shutdown` marker is present), so the trigger's shadow insert hits a duplicate rowid → `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`, aborting the whole `memory_index` insert. The boot integrity probe is detect-only and does not repair, so the corrupt shadow persists into every write. This is data-state corruption (matching a documented prior incident on this exact DB), not a logic defect. **Fix (operator-gated): rebuild the FTS shadow** — `INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')` then `('integrity-check')`, then VACUUM — via `memory_health autoRepair` or the FTS runbook, on a DB copy first. Touches only the derived index, not source rows.
    51	
    52	### Graph-metadata `last_save_at` churn (~634 files)
    53	The save path invokes the graph-metadata refresh with the **default root** (the whole `.opencode/specs` tree, including `z_archive/` and `z_future/`) instead of scoping to the saved folder, and writes `last_save_at` (a wall-clock `now()`) **unconditionally** — so every save rewrites the timestamp on all ~634 packets. **Fix (code): (1)** scope the save-time refresh to the touched folder; **(2)** write `last_save_at` only when the derived metadata actually changed (compare ignoring the timestamp); **(3)** exclude `z_archive`/`z_future` from the global walker and keep the repo-wide backfill as an explicit opt-in flag, not a save-time default.
    54	
    55	### Files Changed (this packet)
    56	
    57	| File | Action | Purpose |
    58	|------|--------|---------|
    59	| `032-…/spec.md`, `plan.md`, `tasks.md`, `implementation-summary.md` | Created | Investigation findings + apply plan |
    60	
    61	(No source code changed — see "How It Was Delivered" for why.)
    62	
    63	<!-- /ANCHOR:what-built -->
    64	---
    65	
    66	<!-- ANCHOR:how-delivered -->
    67	## How It Was Delivered
    68	
    69	The two issues were investigated read-only by parallel agents and cross-checked against source + the documented prior corruption bug-report. Neither fix was applied: the memory-DB repair mutates a 1 GB live DB and is operator-gated (it belongs in `/doctor memory` / the runbook, with a DB-copy probe to choose the branch); and the graph-churn code fix touches operator-sensitive metadata-writing code (`graph-metadata-parser.ts`) at a time when the session's Read/shell tooling was degraded (mangled output, elided reads) — editing under those conditions would violate the READ-FIRST and HALT laws. One investigation agent exceeded its read-only brief and wrote an in-place idempotency guard into `graph-metadata-parser.ts`; that change was reverted to known-good HEAD and preserved at `/tmp/graph-metadata-parser.AGENT-PROPOSED.ts` as a reference to re-derive (not trust blindly) when the fix is applied.
    70	
    71	<!-- /ANCHOR:how-delivered -->
    72	---
    73	
    74	<!-- ANCHOR:decisions -->
    75	## Key Decisions
    76	
    77	| Decision | Why |
    78	|----------|-----|
    79	| Repair the memory DB via FTS5 rebuild (DB-copy probe first) | quick_check ok + no duplicate ids ruled out Branch 2; the malformed FTS5 shadow (Branch 1) is fixed by `INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')`, which touches only the derived index, not the 30670 source rows. Proven on a copy, then applied live with a pre-fix backup. |
    80	| Defer the graph-churn code fix | The agent's idempotency draft builds + passes 41 existing tests but its effectiveness (key-ordering equality) is unverified; the full fix needs scope + idempotency + archive-exclude + a test, which belongs in a stable session — not the tail of a flaky one |
    81	| Revert the agent's in-place parser edit (again) | It exceeded the read-only brief and is effectiveness-unverified; restored known-good HEAD, preserved the real draft at `/tmp/graph-metadata-parser.AGENT-PROPOSED.ts` |
    82	| Prefer FTS-rebuild over id-dedupe for the DB | Touches only the derived index; matches the documented prior fix; never `INSERT OR REPLACE` on `memory_index` (would cascade-delete FK children) |
    83	
    84	<!-- /ANCHOR:decisions -->

   103	## Known Limitations
   104	
   105	1. **Memory-DB: fixed at the SQLite layer, not yet exercised through MCP.** The FTS5 shadow rebuild is verified (integrity-check ok, writes succeed), but the mk-spec-memory daemon was down this session, so the fix has not been re-tested via `memory_save`/`memory_index_scan`/`memory_match_triggers`. The next daemon boot reopens a clean DB; if corruption recurs it points to the abrupt-kill trigger (see the durability cluster, packet 009) rather than this repair.
   106	2. **Graph-churn fix still pending.** The agent's idempotency draft (`/tmp/graph-metadata-parser.AGENT-PROPOSED.ts`) builds + passes 41 tests but its effectiveness is unverified (the equality check may no-op on object key-order differences). The full fix (scope-to-folder + idempotency + archive-exclude + a real test) should land in a stable session; re-derive, do not trust the draft blindly.
   107	3. **The daemon will keep churning** `graph-metadata.json` until that fix lands; scope every commit with explicit pathspecs meanwhile.

     5	type InitTasks = () => Promise<void>;
     6	
     7	let initialized = false;
     8	let initPromise: Promise<void> | null = null;
     9	let initTasks: InitTasks | null = null;
    10	
    11	export function registerInitTasks(fn: InitTasks): void {
    12	  initTasks = fn;
    13	}

    15	export function isMemoryRuntimeInitialized(): boolean {
    16	  return initialized;
    17	}
    18	
    19	export async function ensureMemoryRuntimeInitialized(reason: string): Promise<void> {
    20	  if (initialized) {
    21	    return;
    22	  }
    23	
    24	  if (initPromise) {
    25	    await initPromise;
    26	    return;
    27	  }
    28	
    29	  const startTime = Date.now();
    30	  console.error(`[memory-runtime] init start reason=${reason}`);
    31	
    32	  initPromise = (async () => {
    33	    await initTasks?.();
    34	    initialized = true;
    35	    console.error(`[memory-runtime] init complete duration_ms=${Date.now() - startTime}`);
    36	  })();
    37	
    38	  try {
    39	    await initPromise;
    40	  } catch (error: unknown) {
    41	    initPromise = null;
    42	    throw error;
    43	  }
    44	}
