  1270	function getStartupWorkspaceRoots(basePath: string): string[] {
  1271	  const configuredMemoryRoot = process.env.MEMORY_BASE_PATH?.trim();
  1272	  const derivedMemoryRoot = configuredMemoryRoot ? path.resolve(process.cwd(), configuredMemoryRoot) : null;
  1273	  return Array.from(new Set(
  1274	    [basePath, derivedMemoryRoot, ...ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS]
  1275	      .filter((root): root is string => typeof root === 'string' && root.trim().length > 0)
  1276	      .map((root) => path.resolve(root))
  1277	  ));
  1278	}
  1279	
  1280	function getPendingRecoveryLocations(basePath: string): string[] {
  1281	  const scanLocations: string[] = [];
  1282	  for (const root of getStartupWorkspaceRoots(basePath)) {
  1283	    scanLocations.push(path.join(root, 'specs'));
  1284	    scanLocations.push(path.join(root, '.opencode', 'specs'));
  1285	    const skillDir = path.join(root, '.opencode', 'skills');
  1286	    try {
  1287	      if (!fs.existsSync(skillDir)) continue;
  1288	      for (const entry of fs.readdirSync(skillDir, { withFileTypes: true })) {
  1289	        if (!entry.isDirectory() || entry.name.startsWith('.')) continue;
  1290	        const constDir = path.join(skillDir, entry.name, 'constitutional');
  1291	        if (fs.existsSync(constDir)) scanLocations.push(constDir);
  1292	      }
  1293	    } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1294	      // Non-fatal: constitutional directory discovery failed
  1295	    }
  1296	  }
  1297	  return Array.from(new Set(scanLocations.filter((location) => fs.existsSync(location))));
  1298	}
  1299	
  1300	/**
  1301	 * Recover pending memory files on MCP startup.
  1302	 * Pending files processed by recovery job on next startup.
  1303	 *
  1304	 * Scans for files with _pending suffix (created when index failed after file write)
  1305	 * and attempts to index them.
  1306	 */
  1307	async function recoverPendingFiles(basePath: string): Promise<PendingRecoveryResult> {
  1308	  console.error('[context-server] Checking for pending memory files...');
  1309	
  1310	  try {
  1311	    // BUG-028 FIX: Restrict scan to known memory file locations to prevent OOM when scanning large workspaces.
  1312	    // Share the same allowed-root expansion that startup indexing uses.
  1313	    const existingScanLocations = getPendingRecoveryLocations(basePath);
  1314	
  1315	    // P1 FIX: Wire isCommittedInDb callback so stale pending files are detected at startup.
  1316	    // VectorIndex.getDb() is an initializing accessor — always returns a valid DB after initializeDb().
  1317	    const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
  1318	    const isCommittedInDb = (originalPath: string): boolean => {
  1319	      const row = (database.prepare('SELECT 1 FROM memory_index WHERE file_path = ?') as import('better-sqlite3').Statement).get(originalPath);
  1320	      return !!row;
  1321	    };
  1322	
  1323	    const rawResults = existingScanLocations.flatMap((location) =>
  1324	      transactionManager.recoverAllPendingFiles(location, isCommittedInDb)
  1325	    );
  1326	
  1327	    // Aggregate per-file results into a summary
  1328	    const found = rawResults.length;
  1329	    const recovered = rawResults.filter(r => r.recovered).length;
  1330	    const failed = found - recovered;
  1331	
  1332	    const recoveryResult: PendingRecoveryResult = {
  1333	      found,
  1334	      processed: found,
  1335	      recovered,
  1336	      failed,
  1337	      results: rawResults,
  1338	    };
  1339	
  1340	    if (recoveryResult.found > 0) {
  1341	      console.error(`[context-server] Pending file recovery: ${recoveryResult.recovered} recovered, ${recoveryResult.failed} failed (${recoveryResult.found} total)`);
  1342	    } else {
  1343	      console.error('[context-server] No pending memory files found');
  1344	    }
  1345	
  1346	    return recoveryResult;
  1347	  } catch (error: unknown) {
  1348	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
  1349	    console.error(`[context-server] Pending file recovery error: ${message}`);
  1350	    return { found: 0, processed: 0, recovered: 0, failed: 0, results: [] };
  1351	  }
  1352	}
  1353	
  1354	async function startupScan(basePath: string): Promise<void> {
  1355	  if (startupScanInProgress) {
  1356	    console.error('[context-server] Startup scan already in progress, skipping');
  1357	    return;
  1358	  }
  1359	
  1360	  startupScanInProgress = true;
  1361	  try {
  1362	    // Recover any pending files from previous failed index operations
  1363	    await recoverPendingFiles(basePath);
  1364	
  1365	    console.error('[context-server] Starting background scan for spec documents and constitutional memories...');
  1366	    const scanRoots = Array.from(
  1367	      new Set(
  1368	        [basePath, ...ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS]
  1369	          .filter((root): root is string => typeof root === 'string' && root.trim().length > 0)
  1370	          .map((root) => path.resolve(root))
  1371	      )
  1372	    );
  1373	    const allFiles: string[] = [];
  1374	    const seen = new Set<string>();
  1375	
  1376	    for (const root of scanRoots) {
  1377	      try {
  1378	        const rootFiles = [
  1379	          ...memoryIndexDiscovery.findConstitutionalFiles(root),
  1380	          ...memoryIndexDiscovery.findSpecDocuments(root),
  1381	        ];
  1382	
  1383	        for (const filePath of rootFiles) {
  1384	          const resolved = path.resolve(filePath);
  1385	          if (!seen.has(resolved)) {
  1386	            seen.add(resolved);
  1387	            allFiles.push(filePath);
  1388	          }
  1389	        }
  1390	      } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1391	        // Non-fatal: skip inaccessible startup roots.
  1392	      }
  1393	    }
  1394	
  1395	    if (allFiles.length === 0) {
  1396	      console.error('[context-server] No spec documents or constitutional memories found in workspace');
  1397	      return;
  1398	    }
  1399	
  1400	    console.error(`[context-server] Found ${allFiles.length} indexable files, checking for changes...`);
  1401	    let indexed = 0, updated = 0, unchanged = 0, failed = 0;
  1402	
  1403	    for (const filePath of allFiles) {
  1404	      try {
  1405	        const result: IndexResult = await indexSingleFile(filePath, false);
  1406	        if (result.status === 'indexed') indexed++;
  1407	        else if (result.status === 'updated') updated++;
  1408	        else unchanged++;
  1409	      } catch (error: unknown) {
  1410	        failed++;
  1411	        const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
  1412	        console.error(`[context-server] Failed to index ${path.basename(filePath)}: ${message}`);
  1413	      }
  1414	    }
  1415	
  1416	    if (indexed > 0 || updated > 0) {
  1417	      console.error(`[context-server] Startup scan: ${indexed} new, ${updated} updated, ${unchanged} unchanged, ${failed} failed`);
  1418	    } else {
  1419	      console.error(`[context-server] Startup scan: all ${unchanged} files up to date`);
  1420	    }
  1421	
  1422	    if (indexed > 0 || updated > 0) {
  1423	      try {
  1424	        runPostMutationHooks('scan', {
  1425	          indexed,
  1426	          updated,
  1427	          staleDeleted: 0,
  1428	          staleDeleteFailed: 0,
  1429	          operation: 'startup-scan',
  1430	        });
  1431	      } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1432	        // Non-fatal: startup scan must continue even if invalidation hooks fail.
  1433	      }
  1434	    }
  1435	
  1436	    // Log atomicity metrics for monitoring
  1437	    const metrics = transactionManager.getMetrics();
  1438	    if (metrics.totalRecoveries > 0 || metrics.totalErrors > 0) {
  1439	      console.error(`[context-server] Atomicity metrics: ${metrics.totalAtomicWrites} successful, ${metrics.totalErrors} failed, ${metrics.totalRecoveries} recovered`);
  1440	    }
  1441	  } catch (error: unknown) {
  1442	    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
  1443	    console.error(`[context-server] Startup scan error: ${message}`);
  1444	  } finally {
  1445	    startupScanInProgress = false;
  1446	  }
  1447	}
  1448

  1450	   7. GRACEFUL SHUTDOWN
  1620	/* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1621	   8. MAIN
  1622	──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */

  1624	async function main(): Promise<void> {
  1625	  // Node version mismatch detection (non-blocking)
  1626	  detectNodeVersionMismatch();

  1628	  console.error(
  1629	    `[context-server] Detected runtime: ${detectedRuntime.runtime} (hookPolicy=${detectedRuntime.hookPolicy})`,
  1630	  );
  1631	
  1632	  validateConfiguredEmbeddingsProvider();

  1634	  registerInitTasks(async () => {
  1635	    console.error('[context-server] Initializing database...');
  1636	    const startupDb = vectorIndex.initializeDb();
  1637	    console.error('[context-server] Database initialized');
  1638	    console.error('[context-server] Database path: ' + DATABASE_PATH);

  1648	    console.error(`[context-server] FATAL: ${message}`);
  1649	    process.exit(1);
  1650	  }
  1651	
  1652	  // Pre-Flight API Key Validation
  1653	  // Validates API key at startup to fail fast with actionable error messages
  1654	  // Skip validation if SPECKIT_SKIP_API_VALIDATION=true (for testing/CI)
  1655	  let startupEmbeddingConfig: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof resolveStartupEmbeddingConfig>> | null = null;

  1657	    console.error('[context-server] Validating embedding API key...');

  1659	      startupEmbeddingConfig = await resolveStartupEmbeddingConfig({ timeout: API_KEY_VALIDATION_TIMEOUT_MS });
  1660	      const validation: ApiKeyValidation = startupEmbeddingConfig.validation;

  1749	      console.error(`[context-server] =========================================`);
  1750	      console.error('[context-server] FATAL: Refusing to start with mismatched embedding dimensions');
  1751	      throw new Error(dimValidation.warning ?? 'Embedding dimension mismatch between provider and database');
  1752	    } else if (dimValidation.stored) {
  1753	      console.error(`[context-server] Embedding dimension validated: ${dimValidation.stored}`);
  1754	    }
  1755	
  1756	    const database = vectorIndex.getDb();
  1757	    if (!database) {
  1758	      throw new Error('Database not initialized after initializeDb(). Cannot start server.');
  1759	    }
  1760	
  1761	    try {
  1762	      initScoringObservability(database);
  1763	      console.error('[context-server] Scoring observability initialized');
  1764	    } catch (scoringObsErr: unknown) {
  1765	      const message = scoringObsErr instanceof Error ? scoringObsErr.message : String(scoringObsErr);
  1766	      console.warn('[context-server] Scoring observability init failed (non-fatal):', message);
  1767	    }
  1768	
  1769	    if (isLearnedFeedbackEnabled()) {
  1770	      try {
  1968	      if (ingestInit.resetCount > 0) {
  1969	        console.error(`[context-server] Ingest crash recovery reset ${ingestInit.resetCount} incomplete job(s) to queued`);
  1970	      }
  1971	    } catch (ingestInitErr: unknown) {
  1972	      const message = ingestInitErr instanceof Error ? ingestInitErr.message : String(ingestInitErr);
  1973	      console.warn('[context-server] Ingest queue init failed:', message);
  1974	    }
  1975	
  1976	    // Optional real-time markdown watcher for automatic re-indexing.
  1977	    if (isFileWatcherEnabled()) {
  1978	      try {
  1979	        const watchPaths = getSpecsBasePaths(DEFAULT_BASE_PATH);
  1980	        if (watchPaths.length > 0) {
  1981	          fileWatcher = startFileWatcher({
  1982	            paths: watchPaths,
  1983	            reindexFn: async (filePath: string) => {
  1984	              const result = await indexSingleFile(filePath, false);
  1985	              if (isMutationStatus(result.status)) {
  1986	                try {
  1987	                  runPostMutationHooks('scan', {
  1988	                    indexed: result.status === 'indexed' || result.status === 'deferred' ? 1 : 0,
  1989	                    updated: result.status === 'updated' || result.status === 'reinforced' ? 1 : 0,
  1990	                    staleDeleted: 0,
  1991	                    staleDeleteFailed: 0,
  1992	                    operation: 'file-watcher-reindex',
  1993	                    filePath,
  1994	                    status: result.status,
  1995	                  });
  1996	                } catch (_error: unknown) {
  1997	                  // Non-fatal by design for file-watcher callback path.
  1998	                }
  1999	              }
  2000	            },
  2001	            removeFn: async (filePath: string) => {
  2002	              await removeIndexedMemoriesForFile(filePath);
  2003	            },
  2004	          });
  2005	          console.error(`[context-server] File watcher started for ${watchPaths.length} path(s)`);
  2006	        } else {
  2007	          console.warn('[context-server] File watcher enabled, but no spec directories were found');
  2008	        }
  2009	      } catch (watchErr: unknown) {
  2010	        const message = watchErr instanceof Error ? watchErr.message : String(watchErr);
  2011	        console.warn('[context-server] File watcher startup failed:', message);
  2012	      }
  2013	
  2014	    }
  2015	  } catch (err: unknown) {
  2016	    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
  2017	    console.error('[context-server] Integrity check failed:', message);
  2018	    throw err instanceof Error ? err : new Error(message);
  2019	  }
  2020	
  2021	    // Background startup scan
  2022	    setImmediate(() => {
  2023	      void startupScan(DEFAULT_BASE_PATH);
  2024	    });
  2025	  });

   243	    ...(maybePostMutationHooks && typeof maybePostMutationHooks === 'object'
   244	      ? { postMutationHooks: maybePostMutationHooks as AtomicSaveResult['postMutationHooks'] }
   245	      : {}),
   246	    ...(hints.length > 0 ? { hints } : {}),
   247	  };
   248	}
   249	
   250	function buildRetryFailureResult(
   251	  filePath: string,
   252	  indexError: Error | null,
   253	  rollbackSucceeded: boolean,
   254	  errorMetadata: Record<string, string> | null,
   255	): AtomicSaveResult {
   256	  const rollbackError = errorMetadata?.rollbackError ? ` (rollback error: ${errorMetadata.rollbackError})` : '';
   257	  const finalErrorMetadata = errorMetadata ?? {};
   258	
   259	  return {
   260	    success: false,
   261	    filePath,
   262	    status: 'error',
   263	    summary: rollbackSucceeded
   264	      ? 'Atomic index failed after retry and restored original file state'
   265	      : 'Atomic index failed after retry and rollback was incomplete',
   266	    message: indexError?.message ?? 'Indexing failed after retry',
   267	    hints: [
   268	      rollbackSucceeded
   269	        ? 'Original file content was preserved or restored because indexing failed before completion'
   270	        : 'Pending file cleanup failed after indexing error; manual cleanup may be required',
   271	      'Retry memory_save({ filePath, force: true }) once dependencies are healthy',
   272	    ],
   273	    error: `Indexing failed after retry${rollbackError}: ${indexError?.message ?? 'unknown'}`,
   274	    ...(Object.keys(finalErrorMetadata).length > 0 ? { errorMetadata: finalErrorMetadata } : {}),
   275	  };
   276	}

   342	export function percentile(values, p) {
   343	  if (values.length === 0) return 0;
   344	  const sorted = [...values].sort((a, b) => a - b);
   345	  const index = Math.ceil((p / 100) * sorted.length) - 1;

     7	const SOCKET_FILE_NAME = 'daemon-ipc.sock';

     9	const MAX_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS = 6999;
    10	const DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS = 150000;
    11	const JSON_RPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION = '2025-06-18';

    15	function parsePositiveInteger(value, fallback) {
    16	  if (value === undefined || value === null || String(value).trim() === '') return fallback;
    17	  const parsed = Number.parseInt(String(value).trim(), 10);
    18	  return Number.isFinite(parsed) && parsed > 0 ? parsed : fallback;
    19	}
    20	
    21	function clampProbeTimeoutMs(value) {
    22	  return Math.min(value, MAX_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS);
    23	}
    24	
    25	function resolveProbeTimeoutMs(options = {}) {
    26	  if (Number.isFinite(options.timeoutMs) && options.timeoutMs > 0) {
    27	    return clampProbeTimeoutMs(options.timeoutMs);
    28	  }
    29	  return clampProbeTimeoutMs(parsePositiveInteger(
    30	    (options.env ?? process.env).SPECKIT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS,
    31	    DEFAULT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS,
    32	  ));
    33	}

    35	function repoRoot() {
    36	  return path.resolve(__dirname, '..', '..', '..');
    37	}
    38	
    39	function defaultDbDirForService(serviceName) {
    40	  const root = repoRoot();
    41	  if (serviceName === 'mk-spec-memory') {
    42	    return path.join(root, '.opencode', 'skills', 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'database');
    43	  }
    44	  if (serviceName === 'mk-code-index') {
    45	    return process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR
    46	      ? path.resolve(process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_DB_DIR)
    47	      : path.join(root, '.opencode', '.spec-kit', 'code-graph', 'database');
    48	  }
    49	  if (serviceName === 'mk-skill-advisor') {
    50	    const advisorOverride = process.env.MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR ?? process.env.SYSTEM_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR;
    51	    return advisorOverride
    52	      ? path.resolve(advisorOverride)
    53	      : path.join(root, '.opencode', 'skills', 'system-skill-advisor', 'mcp_server', 'database');
    54	  }
    55	  throw new Error(`Unknown MCP service name: ${serviceName}`);
    56	}
    57	
    58	function getIpcSocketPath(serviceName, options = {}) {
    59	  if (process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR?.startsWith('tcp://')) {
    60	    return process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR;
    61	  }
    62	  const socketDir = process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR
    63	    ? path.resolve(process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR)
    64	    : path.resolve(options.dbDir ?? defaultDbDirForService(serviceName));
    65	  return path.join(socketDir, SOCKET_FILE_NAME);
    66	}
    67	
    68	function toConnectionOptions(socketPath) {
    69	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
    70	    return socketPath;
    71	  }
    72	  const url = new URL(socketPath);
    73	  return {
    74	    host: url.hostname,
    75	    port: Number.parseInt(url.port, 10),
    76	  };
    77	}
    78	
    79	function bridgeStdioToSocket(socketPath, options = {}) {
    80	  const input = options.stdin ?? process.stdin;
    81	  const output = options.stdout ?? process.stdout;
    82	  const exit = options.exit ?? ((code) => process.exit(code));
    83	  const socket = net.createConnection(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
    84	  let closed = false;
    85	
    86	  const closeOnce = (code = 0) => {
    87	    if (closed) return;
    88	    closed = true;
    89	    input.unpipe(socket);
    90	    socket.unpipe(output);
    91	    socket.destroy();
    92	    exit(code);
    93	  };
    94	
    95	  socket.once('connect', () => {
    96	    options.onConnect?.(socket);
    97	    input.pipe(socket);
    98	    socket.pipe(output);
    99	  });
   100	
   101	  socket.once('error', (error) => {
   102	    options.onError?.(error);
   103	    closeOnce(0);
   104	  });
   105	
   106	  socket.once('close', () => {
   107	    closeOnce(0);
   108	  });
   109	
   110	  input.once('end', () => {
   111	    socket.end();
   112	  });
   113	  input.once('close', () => {
   114	    closeOnce(0);
   115	  });
   116	  output.once('error', () => {
   117	    closeOnce(0);
   118	  });
   119	
   120	  return socket;
   121	}
   122	
   123	function probeDaemon(socketPath, options = {}) {
   124	  const timeoutMs = resolveProbeTimeoutMs(options);
   125	  const connect = options.connect ?? ((connectionOptions) => net.createConnection(connectionOptions));
   126	  // deepProbe=true REQUIRES a JSON-RPC initialize reply to call the daemon alive (detects a hung daemon
   127	  // that accepts the connection but never responds). Without it, a bare socket 'connect' counts as alive
   128	  // (connect-ok) — only safe for callers that genuinely need connection-liveness, NOT for the reap/bridge
   129	  // decision (which always passes deepProbe:true). See maybeBridgeLeaseHolder.
   130	  const deepProbe = options.deepProbe === true;
   131	  const id = nextProbeId++;
   132	  const request = `${JSON.stringify({
   133	    jsonrpc: '2.0',
   134	    id,
   135	    method: 'initialize',
   136	    params: {
   137	      protocolVersion: JSON_RPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
   138	      capabilities: {},
   139	      clientInfo: { name: 'liveness-probe', version: '0' },
   140	    },
   141	  })}\n`;
   142	
   143	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   144	    let socket;
   145	    let settled = false;
   146	    let buffer = '';
   147	    let timer;
   148	
   149	    const finish = (status, reason) => {
   150	      if (settled) return;
   151	      settled = true;
   152	      if (timer) clearTimeout(timer);
   153	      if (socket) socket.destroy();
   154	      resolve({ status, reason });
   155	    };
   156	
   157	    try {
   158	      socket = connect(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
   159	    } catch (error) {
   160	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'connect-threw');
   161	      return;
   162	    }
   163	
   164	    timer = setTimeout(() => finish('dead', 'timeout'), timeoutMs);
   165	    timer.unref?.();
   166	
   167	    socket.once('connect', () => {
   168	      if (!deepProbe) {
   169	        finish('alive', 'connect-ok');
   170	        return;
   171	      }
   172	      socket.write(request);
   173	    });
   174	    socket.on('data', (chunk) => {
   175	      buffer += Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk.toString('utf8') : String(chunk ?? '');
   176	      let newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
   177	      while (newlineIndex !== -1) {
   178	        const line = buffer.slice(0, newlineIndex).trim();
   179	        buffer = buffer.slice(newlineIndex + 1);
   180	        newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
   181	        if (!line) continue;
   182	        try {
   183	          const parsed = JSON.parse(line);
   184	          if (
   185	            parsed &&
   186	            parsed.jsonrpc === '2.0' &&
   187	            parsed.id === id &&
   188	            (Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(parsed, 'result') ||
   189	              Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(parsed, 'error'))
   190	          ) {
   191	            finish('alive', 'json-rpc-reply');
   192	            return;
   193	          }
   194	        } catch {
   195	          // Ignore malformed or unrelated frames; the bounded probe decides liveness.
   196	        }
   197	      }
   198	    });
   199	    socket.once('error', (error) => {
   200	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'socket-error');
   201	    });
   202	    socket.once('close', () => {
   203	      finish('dead', 'closed-before-reply');
   204	    });
   205	  });
   206	}
   207	
   208	function parseHttpJsonResponse(buffer) {
   209	  const raw = buffer.toString('utf8');
   210	  const headerEnd = raw.indexOf('\r\n\r\n');
   211	  if (headerEnd === -1) return null;
   212	  const statusLine = raw.slice(0, raw.indexOf('\r\n'));
   213	  const statusMatch = statusLine.match(/^HTTP\/\d(?:\.\d)?\s+(\d+)/);
   214	  if (!statusMatch) return null;
   215	  const statusCode = Number.parseInt(statusMatch[1], 10);
   216	  const bodyRaw = raw.slice(headerEnd + 4).trim();
   217	  let body = null;
   218	  try {
   219	    body = bodyRaw ? JSON.parse(bodyRaw) : null;
   220	  } catch {
   221	    return { statusCode, body: null };
   222	  }
   223	  return { statusCode, body };
   224	}
   225	
   226	function probeModelServer(socketPath, options = {}) {
   227	  const timeoutMs = resolveProbeTimeoutMs(options);
   228	  const loadingMaxMs = Number.isFinite(options.loadingMaxMs) && options.loadingMaxMs > 0
   229	    ? options.loadingMaxMs
   230	    : parsePositiveInteger(
   231	      (options.env ?? process.env).SPECKIT_HF_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
   232	      DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
   233	    );
   234	  const nowMs = typeof options.nowMs === 'function' ? options.nowMs : () => Date.now();
   235	  const connect = options.connect ?? ((connectionOptions) => net.createConnection(connectionOptions));
   236	  const request = `GET ${MODEL_SERVER_HEALTH_PATH} HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: localhost\r\nAccept: application/json\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n`;
   237	
   238	  return new Promise((resolve) => {
   239	    let socket;
   240	    let settled = false;
   240	    let settled = false;
   241	    let chunks = [];
   242	    let timer;
   243	
   244	    // Carry the parsed health body so callers (the idle-eviction tick) can read
   245	    // lastSuccessfulEmbedAt / inFlight. The 'alive' branches below pass parsed.body as the 3rd arg.
   246	    const finish = (status, reason, health) => {
   247	      if (settled) return;
   248	      settled = true;
   249	      if (timer) clearTimeout(timer);
   250	      if (socket) socket.destroy();
   251	      resolve({ status, reason, health });
   252	    };
   253	
   254	    try {
   255	      socket = connect(toConnectionOptions(socketPath));
   256	    } catch (error) {
   257	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'connect-threw');
   258	      return;
   259	    }
   260	
   261	    timer = setTimeout(() => finish('dead', 'timeout'), timeoutMs);
   262	    timer.unref?.();
   263	
   264	    socket.once('connect', () => {
   265	      socket.write(request);
   266	    });
   267	    socket.on('data', (chunk) => {
   268	      chunks.push(Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk : Buffer.from(String(chunk ?? '')));
   269	    });
   270	    socket.once('end', () => {
   271	      const parsed = parseHttpJsonResponse(Buffer.concat(chunks));
   272	      const state = parsed && parsed.body && typeof parsed.body === 'object'
   273	        ? parsed.body.state
   274	        : null;
   275	      if (parsed && parsed.statusCode >= 200 && parsed.statusCode < 300 && state === 'ready') {
   276	        finish('alive', `health-${state}`, parsed.body);
   277	        return;
   278	      }
   279	      if (parsed && parsed.statusCode >= 200 && parsed.statusCode < 300 && state === 'loading') {
   280	        const loadStartedAt = parsed.body.loadStartedAt;
   281	        const loadProgressAt = parsed.body.loadProgressAt;
   282	        // The loading budget is per model-load attempt; device fallback re-stamps loadProgressAt.
   283	        const loadingMarker = Number.isFinite(loadProgressAt) && loadProgressAt > 0
   284	          ? loadProgressAt
   285	          : loadStartedAt;
   286	        if (Number.isFinite(loadingMarker) && loadingMarker > 0 && nowMs() - loadingMarker > loadingMaxMs) {
   287	          finish('dead', 'loading-wedged');
   288	          return;
   289	        }
   290	        finish('alive', 'health-loading', parsed.body);
   291	        return;
   292	      }
   293	      finish('dead', state === 'error' ? 'health-error' : 'health-not-ready');
   294	    });
   295	    socket.once('error', (error) => {
   296	      finish('dead', error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'socket-error');
   297	    });
   298	    socket.once('close', () => {
   299	      if (!settled && chunks.length === 0) finish('dead', 'closed-before-reply');
   300	    });
   301	  });
   302	}
   303	
   304	async function maybeBridgeLeaseHolder(options) {
   305	  const {
   306	    serviceName,
   307	    leaseResult,
   308	    loggerPrefix,
   309	    dbDir,
   310	    legacyReport,
   311	    probeTimeoutMs,
   312	    connect,
   313	    bridge,
   314	  } = options;
   315	  const startedAt = leaseResult.startedAt ?? new Date(0).toISOString();
   316	  const legacyMarker = leaseResult.legacyPath ? ' (legacy path)' : '';
   317	  const ownerPid = leaseResult.ownerPid;
   318	  const writeLeaseHeld = (suffix = '') => {
   319	    process.stdout.write(`LEASE_HELD_BY:${ownerPid} startedAt=${startedAt}${legacyMarker}${suffix}\n`);
   320	  };
   321	
   322	  if (process.env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED === '1') {
   323	    if (legacyReport) {
   324	      legacyReport(leaseResult);
   325	      return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
   326	    }
   327	    writeLeaseHeld();
   328	    return { action: 'report', reason: 'bridge-disabled' };
   329	  }
   330	
   331	  const socketPath = getIpcSocketPath(serviceName, { dbDir });
   332	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://') && !fs.existsSync(socketPath)) {
   333	    writeLeaseHeld(' (no-bridge-socket)');
   334	    return { action: 'report', reason: 'no-bridge-socket', socketPath };
   335	  }
   336	
   337	  // deepProbe: REQUIRE a JSON-RPC reply, not just a socket accept. The reap/bridge decision must
   338	  // detect a HUNG daemon (event loop wedged / deadlocked — it still accepts the UDS connection but
   339	  // never services requests). A connect-ok-only probe would bridge a client to the wedged daemon and
   340	  // hang it forever, never respawning. The raised probe timeout (default 5000ms, < the 7000ms launcher
   341	  // grace) already prevents false-reaping a busy-but-responsive daemon mid-FTS-merge, so deep probing
   342	  // does not regress that.
   343	  const probe = await probeDaemon(socketPath, { timeoutMs: probeTimeoutMs, connect, deepProbe: true });
   344	  if (probe.status !== 'alive') {
   345	    process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath} failed liveness probe: ${probe.reason}\n`);
   346	    return { action: 'respawn', reason: probe.reason, socketPath };
   347	  }
   348	
   349	  process.stderr.write(`[${loggerPrefix}] bridging to lease holder pid=${ownerPid} socket=${socketPath}\n`);
   350	  const bridgeToSocket = bridge ?? bridgeStdioToSocket;
   351	  bridgeToSocket(socketPath, {
   352	    onError: () => {
   353	      writeLeaseHeld(' (bridge-refused)');
   354	    },
   355	  });
   356	  return { action: 'bridge', socketPath };
   357	}
   358	
   359	module.exports = {
   360	  DEFAULT_MODEL_SERVER_LOADING_MAX_MS,
   361	  bridgeStdioToSocket,
   362	  getIpcSocketPath,
   363	  maybeBridgeLeaseHolder,
   364	  probeDaemon,
   365	  probeModelServer,
   366	  toConnectionOptions,
   367	};

    21	---
    22	
    23	## 2. OWNERSHIP
    24	
    25	| Surface | Owner | Notes |
    26	|---|---|---|
    27	| Socket bridge | Spec Kit Memory MCP | Bridges secondary clients to the active MCP server. |
    28	| Idle monitor | Shared launcher pattern, spec-memory copy | Tracks stdio and IPC activity through `SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN`. |
    29	| Lease cleanup | Launcher process | Existing child-process exit handlers clear launcher lease files after server exit. |
    30	
    31	---
    32	
    33	## 3. KEY FILES
    34	
    35	| File | Responsibility |
    36	|---|---|
    37	| `socket-server.ts` | Resolves socket paths, starts the secondary-client bridge, tracks activity, and exposes bridge stats. |
    38	| `launcher-idle-timeout.ts` | Parses `SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN` and runs the idle shutdown timer. |
    39	
    40	---
    41	
    42	## 4. BOUNDARIES
    43	
    44	- Keep transport, socket, and idle-monitor behavior here.
    45	- Keep MCP handler logic outside IPC helpers.
    46	- Keep the LaunchAgent and orphan sweeper runbook in `.opencode/scripts/README.md`.
    47	
    48	---

     1	// MODULE: Launcher Idle Timeout Monitor
     2	
     3	const DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN = 30;
     4	const MS_PER_MINUTE = 60_000;
     5	const MIN_IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS = 1_000;
     6	const MAX_IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS = 60_000;
     7	
     8	type IntervalHandle = ReturnType<typeof setInterval>;
     9	type DataListener = (chunk: unknown) => void;
    10	type StdinLike = {
    11	  on(eventName: 'data', listener: DataListener): unknown;
    12	  off?: (eventName: 'data', listener: DataListener) => unknown;
    13	  removeListener?: (eventName: 'data', listener: DataListener) => unknown;
    14	};
    15	
    16	interface LauncherIdleMonitorOptions {
    17	  readonly serviceName: string;
    18	  readonly timeoutMinutesRaw?: string;
    19	  readonly stdin?: StdinLike | null;
    20	  readonly getActiveClientCount?: () => number;
    21	  readonly onIdle: () => void | Promise<void>;
    22	  readonly log?: (message: string) => void;
    23	  readonly now?: () => number;
    24	  readonly setIntervalFn?: (callback: () => void, ms: number) => IntervalHandle;
    25	  readonly clearIntervalFn?: (timer: IntervalHandle) => void;
    26	}
    27	
    28	interface LauncherIdleMonitor {
    29	  readonly timeoutMs: number | null;
    30	  readonly markActivity: () => void;
    31	  readonly stop: () => void;
    32	}
    33	
    34	function parseLauncherIdleTimeoutMs(rawValue = process.env.SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN): number | null {
    35	  const raw = rawValue === undefined || rawValue === null || String(rawValue).trim() === ''
    36	    ? String(DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN)
    37	    : String(rawValue).trim();
    38	  const parsedMinutes = Number(raw);
    39	  if (!Number.isFinite(parsedMinutes) || parsedMinutes < 0) {
    40	    return DEFAULT_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MIN * MS_PER_MINUTE;
    41	  }
    42	  if (parsedMinutes === 0) {
    43	    return null;
    44	  }
    45	  return Math.max(1, Math.round(parsedMinutes * MS_PER_MINUTE));
    46	}
    47	
    48	function removeDataListener(stdin: StdinLike | null | undefined, listener: DataListener): void {
    49	  if (!stdin) return;
    50	  if (typeof stdin.off === 'function') {
    51	    stdin.off('data', listener);
    52	    return;
    53	  }
    54	  if (typeof stdin.removeListener === 'function') {
    55	    stdin.removeListener('data', listener);
    56	  }
    57	}
    58	
    59	function createLauncherIdleMonitor(options: LauncherIdleMonitorOptions): LauncherIdleMonitor {
    60	  const log = options.log ?? ((message: string) => console.error(message));
    61	  const timeoutMs = parseLauncherIdleTimeoutMs(options.timeoutMinutesRaw);
    62	  const noop = () => undefined;
    63	  if (timeoutMs === null) {
    64	    log(`[${options.serviceName}] launcher idle timeout disabled`);
    65	    return { timeoutMs, markActivity: noop, stop: noop };
    66	  }
    67	
    68	  const now = options.now ?? (() => Date.now());
    69	  const setIntervalFn = options.setIntervalFn ?? ((callback: () => void, ms: number) => setInterval(callback, ms));
    70	  const clearIntervalFn = options.clearIntervalFn ?? ((interval: IntervalHandle) => clearInterval(interval));
    71	  const stdin = options.stdin === undefined ? process.stdin : options.stdin;
    72	  const checkIntervalMs = Math.min(
    73	    MAX_IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS,
    74	    Math.max(MIN_IDLE_CHECK_INTERVAL_MS, Math.floor(timeoutMs / 2)),
    75	  );
    76	
    77	  let stopped = false;
    78	  let lastActivityAt = now();
    79	  let timer: IntervalHandle | null = null;
    80	
    81	  const markActivity = () => {
    82	    if (!stopped) {
    83	      lastActivityAt = now();
    84	    }
    85	  };
    86	
    87	  const stop = () => {
    88	    if (stopped) return;
    89	    stopped = true;
    90	    removeDataListener(stdin, markActivity);
    91	    if (timer !== null) {
    92	      clearIntervalFn(timer);
    93	    }
    94	  };
    95	
    96	  const checkIdle = async () => {
    97	    if (stopped) return;
    98	    const activeClients = options.getActiveClientCount?.() ?? 0;
    99	    if (activeClients > 0) {
   100	      lastActivityAt = now();
   101	      return;
   102	    }
   103	    const idleForMs = now() - lastActivityAt;
   104	    if (idleForMs < timeoutMs) return;
   105	
   106	    stopped = true;
   107	    removeDataListener(stdin, markActivity);
   108	    if (timer !== null) {
   109	      clearIntervalFn(timer);
   110	    }
   111	    log(`[${options.serviceName}] launcher idle timeout reached after ${Math.round(idleForMs)}ms`);
   112	    try {
   113	      await options.onIdle();
   114	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   115	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   116	      log(`[${options.serviceName}] launcher idle shutdown failed: ${message}`);
   117	    }
   118	  };
   119	
   120	  stdin?.on('data', markActivity);
   121	  timer = setIntervalFn(() => {
   122	    void checkIdle();
   123	  }, checkIntervalMs);
   124	  if (typeof (timer as { unref?: () => void }).unref === 'function') {
   125	    (timer as { unref: () => void }).unref();
   126	  }
   127	
   128	  return { timeoutMs, markActivity, stop };
   129	}
   130	
   131	export {
   132	  createLauncherIdleMonitor,
   133	  parseLauncherIdleTimeoutMs,
   134	};
   135	
   136	export type {
   137	  LauncherIdleMonitor,
   138	  LauncherIdleMonitorOptions,
   139	};
     1	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     2	// MODULE: IPC Socket Server (multi-client launcher bridge)
     3	// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     4	
     5	import fs from 'node:fs';
     6	import net from 'node:net';
     7	import path from 'node:path';
     8	
     9	import { Server } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js';
    10	import { StdioServerTransport } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js';
    11	
    12	const SOCKET_FILE_NAME = 'daemon-ipc.sock';
    13	const DEFAULT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS = 8;
    14	const TCP_EADDRINUSE_RETRY_DELAYS_MS = [100, 250, 500, 1000, 1500] as const;
    15	
    16	interface IpcBridgeStats {
    17	  socket_path: string | null;
    18	  secondary_clients_count: number;
    19	  total_secondary_messages_in: number;
    20	  total_secondary_messages_out: number;
    21	}
    22	
    23	interface IpcSocketServerOptions {
    24	  readonly socketPath: string;
    25	  readonly createServer: () => Server;
    26	  readonly maxClients?: number;
    27	  readonly log?: (message: string) => void;
    28	  readonly onActivity?: () => void;
    29	}
    30	
    31	interface IpcSocketServerHandle {
    32	  readonly socketPath: string;
    33	  readonly close: () => Promise<void>;
    34	}
    35	
    36	let activeServer: net.Server | null = null;
    37	let activeSocketPath: string | null = null;
    38	const activeSockets = new Set<net.Socket>();
    39	const activeTransports = new Map<net.Socket, StdioServerTransport>();
    40	let totalSecondaryMessagesIn = 0;
    41	let totalSecondaryMessagesOut = 0;
    42	
    43	function countJsonRpcFrames(chunk: unknown): number {
    44	  const text = Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? chunk.toString('utf8') : String(chunk ?? '');
    45	  const newlineCount = text.split('\n').length - 1;
    46	  return Math.max(1, newlineCount);
    47	}
    48	
    49	function parseMaxClients(rawValue = process.env.SPECKIT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS): number {
    50	  if (rawValue === undefined || rawValue === null || String(rawValue).trim() === '') {
    51	    return DEFAULT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS;
    52	  }
    53	  const parsed = Number.parseInt(String(rawValue), 10);
    54	  if (!Number.isFinite(parsed) || parsed < 1) {
    55	    return DEFAULT_MAX_SECONDARY_CLIENTS;
    56	  }
    57	  return parsed;
    58	}
    59	
    60	function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
    61	  return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));
    62	}
    63	
    64	function resolveIpcSocketPath(dbDir: string): string {
    65	  const socketDir = process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR
    66	    ? path.resolve(process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR)
    67	    : path.resolve(dbDir);
    68	  return path.join(socketDir, SOCKET_FILE_NAME);
    69	}
    70	
    71	function getIpcBridgeStats(): IpcBridgeStats {
    72	  return {
    73	    socket_path: activeSocketPath,
    74	    secondary_clients_count: activeSockets.size,
    75	    total_secondary_messages_in: totalSecondaryMessagesIn,
    76	    total_secondary_messages_out: totalSecondaryMessagesOut,
    77	  };
    78	}
    79	
    80	async function listenOnce(server: net.Server, socketPath: string): Promise<void> {
    81	  await new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
    82	    const onError = (error: NodeJS.ErrnoException) => {
    83	      server.off('listening', onListening);
    84	      reject(error);
    85	    };
    86	    const onListening = () => {
    87	      server.off('error', onError);
    88	      resolve();
    89	    };
    90	    server.once('error', onError);
    91	    server.once('listening', onListening);
    92	    if (socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
    93	      const url = new URL(socketPath);
    94	      server.listen(Number.parseInt(url.port, 10), url.hostname);
    95	      return;
    96	    }
    97	    server.listen(socketPath);
    98	  });
    99	}
   100	
   101	async function retryTcpListenAfterEaddrInUse(
   102	  server: net.Server,
   103	  socketPath: string,
   104	  log: (message: string) => void,
   105	): Promise<boolean> {
   106	  for (const delayMs of TCP_EADDRINUSE_RETRY_DELAYS_MS) {
   107	    log(`[ipc-bridge] tcp socket in use at ${socketPath}; retrying listen in ${delayMs}ms`);
   108	    await sleep(delayMs);
   109	    try {
   110	      await listenOnce(server, socketPath);
   111	      return true;
   112	    } catch (error: unknown) {
   113	      const err = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException;
   114	      if (err.code !== 'EADDRINUSE') {
   115	        throw err;
   116	      }
   117	    }
   118	  }
   119	  return false;
   120	}
   121	
   122	async function startIpcSocketServer(options: IpcSocketServerOptions): Promise<IpcSocketServerHandle> {
   123	  const socketPath = options.socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')
   124	    ? options.socketPath
   125	    : path.resolve(options.socketPath);
   126	  const log = options.log ?? ((message: string) => console.error(message));
   127	  const maxClients = options.maxClients ?? parseMaxClients();
   128	  const onActivity = options.onActivity ?? (() => undefined);
   129	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   130	    fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(socketPath), { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
   131	  }
   132	
   133	  const server = net.createServer((socket) => {
   134	    if (activeSockets.size >= maxClients) {
   135	      log(`[ipc-bridge] refusing secondary connection: max clients ${maxClients} reached`);
   136	      socket.end();
   137	      socket.destroy();
   138	      return;
   139	    }
   140	
   141	    activeSockets.add(socket);
   142	    onActivity();
   143	    log('[ipc-bridge] secondary connected pid=?');
   144	
   145	    const originalWrite = socket.write.bind(socket);
   146	    socket.write = ((chunk: unknown, ...args: unknown[]) => {
   147	      onActivity();
   148	      totalSecondaryMessagesOut += countJsonRpcFrames(chunk);
   149	      return originalWrite(chunk as string | Uint8Array, ...(args as [BufferEncoding?, (() => void)?]));
   150	    }) as typeof socket.write;
   151	
   152	    socket.on('data', (chunk) => {
   153	      onActivity();
   154	      totalSecondaryMessagesIn += countJsonRpcFrames(chunk);
   155	    });
   156	
   157	    const transport = new StdioServerTransport(socket, socket);
   158	    activeTransports.set(socket, transport);
   159	    const secondaryServer = options.createServer();
   160	    secondaryServer.connect(transport).catch((error: unknown) => {
   161	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
   162	      log(`[ipc-bridge] secondary connect error: ${message}`);
   163	      socket.destroy();
   164	    });
   165	
   166	    socket.once('close', () => {
   167	      activeSockets.delete(socket);
   168	      activeTransports.delete(socket);
   169	      void transport.close();
   170	      log('[ipc-bridge] disconnect');
   171	    });
   172	    socket.once('error', (error) => {
   173	      log(`[ipc-bridge] socket error: ${error.message}`);
   174	    });
   175	  });
   176	
   177	  try {
   178	    await listenOnce(server, socketPath);
   179	  } catch (error: unknown) {
   180	    const err = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException;
   181	    if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE' && socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   182	      const listened = await retryTcpListenAfterEaddrInUse(server, socketPath, log);
   183	      if (!listened) {
   184	        log(`[ipc-bridge] tcp socket remained in use at ${socketPath}; secondary bridge disabled`);
   185	        return {
   186	          socketPath,
   187	          close: async () => {
   188	            for (const socket of activeSockets) {
   189	              socket.destroy();
   190	            }
   191	            activeSockets.clear();
   192	            activeTransports.clear();
   193	          },
   194	        };
   195	      }
   196	    } else if (err.code !== 'EADDRINUSE') {
   197	      throw err;
   198	    } else {
   199	      try {
   200	        fs.unlinkSync(socketPath);
   201	      } catch (unlinkError: unknown) {
   202	        const unlinkErr = unlinkError as NodeJS.ErrnoException;
   203	        if (unlinkErr.code !== 'ENOENT') {
   204	          throw unlinkErr;
   205	        }
   206	      }
   207	      await listenOnce(server, socketPath);
   208	    }
   209	  }
   210	
   211	  const address = server.address();
   212	  const listenedPath = typeof address === 'object' && address
   213	    ? `tcp://${address.address}:${address.port}`
   214	    : socketPath;
   215	  if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   216	    fs.chmodSync(socketPath, 0o600);
   217	  }
   218	  activeServer = server;
   219	  activeSocketPath = listenedPath;
   220	  log(`[ipc-bridge] socket listening at ${listenedPath}`);
   221	
   222	  return {
   223	    socketPath: listenedPath,
   224	    close: async () => {
   225	      for (const socket of activeSockets) {
   226	        socket.destroy();
   227	      }
   228	      activeSockets.clear();
   229	      activeTransports.clear();
   230	      await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
   231	        server.close(() => resolve());
   232	      });
   233	      if (activeServer === server) {
   234	        activeServer = null;
   235	        activeSocketPath = null;
   236	      }
   237	      if (!socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) {
   238	        try {
   239	          fs.unlinkSync(socketPath);
   240	        } catch (error: unknown) {
   241	          const err = error as NodeJS.ErrnoException;
   242	          if (err.code !== 'ENOENT') {
   243	            throw err;
   244	          }
   245	        }
   246	      }
   247	    },
   248	  };
   249	}
   250	
   251	export {
   252	  getIpcBridgeStats,
   253	  parseMaxClients,
   254	  resolveIpcSocketPath,
   255	  startIpcSocketServer,
   256	};
   257	
   258	export type {
   259	  IpcBridgeStats,
   260	  IpcSocketServerHandle,

.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:49:### Memory-DB `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50:Every memory write funnels through one `INSERT` into `memory_index`, which fires the FTS5 sync trigger `memory_fts_insert` (`INSERT INTO memory_fts(rowid,...) VALUES(new.id,...)`). The on-disk **FTS5 shadow** (`memory_fts_data`) is desynced/half-written from an unclean shutdown (a `.unclean-shutdown` marker is present), so the trigger's shadow insert hits a duplicate rowid → `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY`, aborting the whole `memory_index` insert. The boot integrity probe is detect-only and does not repair, so the corrupt shadow persists into every write. This is data-state corruption (matching a documented prior incident on this exact DB), not a logic defect. **Fix (operator-gated): rebuild the FTS shadow** — `INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')` then `('integrity-check')`, then VACUUM — via `memory_health autoRepair` or the FTS runbook, on a DB copy first. Touches only the derived index, not source rows.
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:79:| Repair the memory DB via FTS5 rebuild (DB-copy probe first) | quick_check ok + no duplicate ids ruled out Branch 2; the malformed FTS5 shadow (Branch 1) is fixed by `INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')`, which touches only the derived index, not the 30670 source rows. Proven on a copy, then applied live with a pre-fix backup. |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:92:| Memory-DB root cause | DONE — corrupted FTS5 shadow + detect-only boot probe; matches prior incident |
.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:105:1. **Memory-DB: fixed at the SQLite layer, not yet exercised through MCP.** The FTS5 shadow rebuild is verified (integrity-check ok, writes succeed), but the mk-spec-memory daemon was down this session, so the fix has not been re-tested via `memory_save`/`memory_index_scan`/`memory_match_triggers`.      1	# Deep Research Charter — Remaining system-spec-kit failures (common-cause)
     2	
     3	## Research Topic
     4	Root-cause the remaining system-spec-kit failures observed after this session's recent phases, and determine whether they share a common underlying cause (daemon-lifecycle / single-writer contention).
     5	
     6	## Executor
     7	cli-codex · gpt-5.5 · reasoning=high · service-tier=fast · timeout 900s. Native @deep-research fallback after 2 consecutive cli failures.
     8	
     9	## Sub-questions (P0 = blocker for the verdict)
    10	- **SQ1 [P0] Substrate 5-vs-4:** Why does `substrate-runner-harness.vitest.ts:42` find 5 verdict rows when it asserts 4? Is it (a) a 5th scenario added without updating the assertion, (b) a duplicate row, or (c) a non-deterministic double-write through the two real MCP daemons? Evidence: the harness, its TSV producer, the scenario list (403/404/407/410).
    11	- **SQ2 [P0] Memory constraint recurrence:** Why does `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY` recur on `memory_index` insert (via the `memory_fts_insert` FTS5 trigger) in a fresh process *after* an FTS rebuild + clean restart, while the at-rest DB is healthy (quick_check ok, memory_index = memory_fts counts)? What makes the daemon exit uncleanly and re-drop `.unclean-shutdown`?
    12	- **SQ3 [P1] Graph-metadata churn:** Confirm the committed root cause (default-root walk + unconditional last_save_at + Zod stripping last_active_child_id/last_active_at) holds, and whether it interacts with the daemon lifecycle.
    13	- **SQ4 [P0] Common cause:** Do SQ2's unclean-shutdown recurrence and SQ1's multi-daemon double-write both trace to the single-writer / daemon-lifecycle contention that 031/009 and 032 addressed (multiple daemons / unclean exits under concurrent sessions)?

+# Iteration 002 - SQ2 memory constraint recurrence and unclean-shutdown lifecycle
+
+## Question
+
+Why does `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY` recur on `memory_index` insert via `memory_fts_insert` after an FTS rebuild and clean restart, and does the SQ1 `Connection closed` diagnostic come from the same daemon-lifecycle path that re-drops `.unclean-shutdown`? The charter names SQ2 as P0 and asks why the constraint recurs while the at-rest DB is healthy enough for `quick_check` and row-count parity `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:11`.
+
+## Investigation (commands run + what each showed)
+
+- `nl -ba .../deep-research-strategy.md`, registry, and `iteration-001.md`: confirmed SQ2 is the highest-value unanswered P0, registry has no known findings, and Iteration 001 answered SQ1 as a runner diagnostic rather than a fifth scenario or double-write `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:11`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-findings-registry.json:1`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/iteration-001.md:22`.
+- `rg -n "unclean-shutdown|shutdown|Connection closed|childPid|lease|spawn|exit" .opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs` plus targeted `nl -ba`: traced the launcher lease, bridge/respawn, child spawn, child-exit supervision, and signal shutdown paths `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:229`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:356`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:763`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:772`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:803`.
+- `rg -n "unclean-shutdown|memory_fts_insert|INSERT INTO memory_index|INSERT INTO memory_fts" .../lib/search` plus targeted `nl -ba`: confirmed the DB marker writer/remover, the `memory_index` insert paths, and the FTS5 trigger that mirrors each inserted row into `memory_fts` `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:739`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1270`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts:264`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts:2452`.
+- `nl -ba .../context-server.ts`: traced memory runtime initialization, boot FTS check, graceful shutdown, and fatal direct-exit paths. The boot FTS check is detect-only, while graceful shutdown calls `vectorIndex.closeDb()` before exit `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:360`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1473`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:2070`.
+- `nl -ba .../memory-crud-health.ts`: confirmed `memory_health` auto-repair is gated and only rebuilds FTS when `memory_index` and `memory_fts` row counts diverge `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:623`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:651`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:662`.
+- `nl -ba .../run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs` and `nl -ba .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs`: traced the SQ1 diagnostic producer and found that bridge-disabled launcher runs can close the client without necessarily opening the DB when a live lease is already held `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:326`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:607`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:327`.
+- `nl -ba .opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.unclean-shutdown` and launcher lease file: read-only observation showed a current marker with DB-writer pid `94516` and a lease file with launcher pid `79793` plus child pid `3423`; process liveness could not be checked because `ps` was denied by the sandbox `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.unclean-shutdown:1`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.mk-spec-memory-launcher.json:2`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.mk-spec-memory-launcher.json:5`.
+- `rg -n "FTS5|SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY|unclean-shutdown" .../032...`: cross-checked packet 032's existing conclusion that the FTS shadow, not `memory_index` data rows, is the failure surface; it also records that a later recurrence after rebuild points back toward abrupt shutdown rather than the repair itself `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:105`.
+- `rg -n "marker|single-writer|live two-launcher" .../031...`: cross-checked packet 031/009; it fixed deterministic single-writer/durability families, but its own limitation says live two-launcher integration remains unproven `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/031-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:47`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/031-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`.
+
+## Findings (each: CONFIRMED|HYPOTHESIS | the claim | evidence file:line | confidence 0-1 | tag fullyNew|partiallyNew|known)
+
+- CONFIRMED | The primary-key failure on write is caused downstream of a normal `memory_index` insert: both embedded and deferred save paths execute `INSERT INTO memory_index`, and the `memory_fts_insert` trigger then inserts `new.id` into `memory_fts(rowid, ...)`. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts:264`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts:363`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts:2452`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts:2455`. Confidence 0.98. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | Row-count parity between `memory_index` and `memory_fts` is not a sufficient health proof for the observed failure mode, because packet 032 identifies the failing state as corrupted FTS5 shadow state (`memory_fts_data`) while the repair decision explicitly ruled out duplicate source IDs and preferred rebuilding only the derived FTS shadow. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:79`. Confidence 0.9. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | `.unclean-shutdown` is written on every non-`:memory:` DB open, before schema/bootstrap work completes, so marker presence after boot does not by itself prove corruption; it means the DB is open or was not closed through the clean path. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:739`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:746`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1270`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1297`. Confidence 0.97. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The marker is removed only from `close_db()` after the active DB runs a main WAL checkpoint, detaches/closes, and reaches the success branch; failed checkpoint/close or skipped `close_db()` leaves the marker in place. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1366`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1369`. Confidence 0.98. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The intended graceful context-server path does clean the DB: `fatalShutdown()` drains the file watcher, calls `vectorIndex.closeDb()`, closes transport/IPC, then exits. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1473`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1493`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1543`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | There are direct fatal-exit paths that bypass `fatalShutdown()` and therefore bypass `vectorIndex.closeDb()`: startup initializes the DB, then API/embedder validation can call `process.exit(1)`, and the top-level `main().catch` also exits directly. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1634`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1636`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1648`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1649`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:2070`. Confidence 0.91. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The boot FTS integrity probe does not repair corruption; it runs only if the marker exists, executes `memory_fts` `integrity-check`, logs a detect-only warning on failure, and attempts no rebuild/swap/deletion. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:360`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:363`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:373`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:380`. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The confirmation-gated `memory_health` repair path is narrower than the recurrence described in SQ2: it plans repair only with explicit `autoRepair` confirmation, and its FTS rebuild branch is reached when `memory_index` and `memory_fts` counts differ. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:623`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:639`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:654`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:656`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:662`. Confidence 0.88. Tag fullyNew.
+- HYPOTHESIS | SQ2's recurrence after a clean rebuild is best explained by a subsequent unclean context-server exit after the DB has opened and possibly written FTS/WAL frames, not by standing source-row corruption. The support is that marker write happens on DB open, clean removal is gated on `close_db()`, direct `process.exit(1)` paths skip that cleanup, and packet 032 says recurrence after rebuild points toward abrupt kill. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1270`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1649`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:105`. Confidence 0.78. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The SQ1 `Connection closed` diagnostic is a symptom of launcher/client connection failure, not proof of DB corruption: the runner catches `client.connect()` failure and records `runner:mk-spec-memory`, and the captured evidence contains only that MCP connection-closed detail. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:309`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:326`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:333`, `_sandbox/24--local-llm-query-intelligence/evidence/run-2026-05-14-shared-daemon.summary.tsv:2`. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | A bridge-disabled launcher run can close the substrate client without opening the DB if a live lease is already held: strict single-writer checks run before `launchServer()`, `SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED=1` reports `LEASE_HELD_BY`, and `main()` returns. Evidence: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:878`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:881`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:882`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:327`. Confidence 0.9. Tag fullyNew.
+- HYPOTHESIS | The same concrete event may produce both SQ1's `Connection closed` and SQ2's marker only when the launcher actually spawns `context-server.js` and that child exits after `vectorIndex.initializeDb()`; the bridge-disabled/live-lease path produces `Connection closed`-class symptoms without a DB open. Evidence for spawn/DB-open path: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:763`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:772`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1636`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1270`; evidence for no-spawn lease path: `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:881`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`. Confidence 0.74. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | Packet 031/009 reduces the common-cause risk but does not close it end-to-end, because it landed deterministic unit-level single-writer/durability fixes while explicitly leaving live two-launcher integration unproven. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/031-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:47`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/031-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:55`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/031-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`. Confidence 0.94. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | Current read-only state shows a marker and a launcher lease, but not enough to prove the marker PID is dead in this sandbox: the marker records pid `94516`, while the lease records launcher pid `79793` and child pid `3423`; process liveness could not be checked. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.unclean-shutdown:1`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.mk-spec-memory-launcher.json:2`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/database/.mk-spec-memory-launcher.json:5`. Confidence 0.72. Tag fullyNew.
+
+## Negative knowledge (directions ruled OUT this iteration, with why)
+
+- Ruled out "marker presence alone means the DB is corrupted." The marker is deliberately written on every DB open and removed only on clean close, so a live process or skipped cleanup can leave it present without proving FTS shadow corruption. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:739`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1270`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`.
+- Ruled out `memory_index = memory_fts` row-count parity as a complete SQ2 repair/health proof. The known failure is in the FTS5 shadow and the repair decision says the malformed shadow branch was chosen after source-row duplicate IDs were ruled out. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:79`.
+- Ruled out the boot integrity check as the mechanism that heals recurrence. The boot check logs `DETECT-ONLY` and explicitly attempts no DB recovery. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:371`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:373`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:374`.
+- Ruled out a necessary one-to-one link between the SQ1 diagnostic row and `.unclean-shutdown`. With `SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED=1`, a live existing lease can make the launcher report and return before `launchServer()`, which can close the MCP client without opening the DB. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:619`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:327`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`.
+- Ruled out treating packet 031/009 as end-to-end proof that live recurrence cannot happen. It verifies deterministic suites but still calls out the absence of a live two-launcher integration run. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/031-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:55`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/031-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`.
+
+## Open questions remaining (which SQ still unanswered)
+
+- SQ2 is partially answered but not fully closed. The code proves how unclean markers recur and identifies direct cleanup-bypass exits, but this iteration did not prove which specific runtime event killed the daemon in the observed recurrence `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:11`.
+- SQ3 remains unanswered; this iteration did not re-check graph-metadata churn or its daemon-lifecycle interaction `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:12`.
+- SQ4 is narrowed but not closed. SQ1 is still not evidence of a two-daemon double-write; the likely common cause is broader launcher/daemon lifecycle cleanup, and the exact overlap with SQ2 depends on whether the connection closed before or after DB initialization `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:13`.
+
+## newInfoRatio self-estimate (0-1) + one-line justification
+
+0.66 - This iteration added concrete marker lifecycle, cleanup-bypass, and bridge-disabled negative knowledge for SQ2/SQ4, but the exact observed daemon death remains a hypothesis rather than a fully reconstructed event.

+# Iteration 002 - SQ2 memory constraint recurrence and unclean-shutdown lifecycle
+
+## Question
+
+Why does `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_PRIMARYKEY` recur on `memory_index` insert via `memory_fts_insert` after an FTS rebuild and clean restart, and does the SQ1 `Connection closed` diagnostic come from the same daemon-lifecycle path that re-drops `.unclean-shutdown`? The charter names SQ2 as P0 and asks why the constraint recurs while the at-rest DB is healthy enough for `quick_check` and row-count parity `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:11`.
+
