+## Findings (each: CONFIRMED|HYPOTHESIS | the claim | evidence file:line | confidence 0-1 | tag fullyNew|partiallyNew|known)
+
+- CONFIRMED | The primary-key failure on write is caused downstream of a normal `memory_index` insert: both embedded and deferred save paths execute `INSERT INTO memory_index`, and the `memory_fts_insert` trigger then inserts `new.id` into `memory_fts(rowid, ...)`. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts:264`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-mutations.ts:363`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts:2452`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-schema.ts:2455`. Confidence 0.98. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | Row-count parity between `memory_index` and `memory_fts` is not a sufficient health proof for the observed failure mode, because packet 032 identifies the failing state as corrupted FTS5 shadow state (`memory_fts_data`) while the repair decision explicitly ruled out duplicate source IDs and preferred rebuilding only the derived FTS shadow. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:79`. Confidence 0.9. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | `.unclean-shutdown` is written on every non-`:memory:` DB open, before schema/bootstrap work completes, so marker presence after boot does not by itself prove corruption; it means the DB is open or was not closed through the clean path. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:739`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:746`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1270`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1297`. Confidence 0.97. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The marker is removed only from `close_db()` after the active DB runs a main WAL checkpoint, detaches/closes, and reaches the success branch; failed checkpoint/close or skipped `close_db()` leaves the marker in place. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1353`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1366`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1369`. Confidence 0.98. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The intended graceful context-server path does clean the DB: `fatalShutdown()` drains the file watcher, calls `vectorIndex.closeDb()`, closes transport/IPC, then exits. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1473`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1493`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1507`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1543`. Confidence 0.96. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | There are direct fatal-exit paths that bypass `fatalShutdown()` and therefore bypass `vectorIndex.closeDb()`: startup initializes the DB, then API/embedder validation can call `process.exit(1)`, and the top-level `main().catch` also exits directly. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1634`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1636`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1648`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1649`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:2070`. Confidence 0.91. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The boot FTS integrity probe does not repair corruption; it runs only if the marker exists, executes `memory_fts` `integrity-check`, logs a detect-only warning on failure, and attempts no rebuild/swap/deletion. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:360`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:363`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:373`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:380`. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The confirmation-gated `memory_health` repair path is narrower than the recurrence described in SQ2: it plans repair only with explicit `autoRepair` confirmation, and its FTS rebuild branch is reached when `memory_index` and `memory_fts` counts differ. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:623`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:639`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:654`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:656`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-crud-health.ts:662`. Confidence 0.88. Tag fullyNew.
+- HYPOTHESIS | SQ2's recurrence after a clean rebuild is best explained by a subsequent unclean context-server exit after the DB has opened and possibly written FTS/WAL frames, not by standing source-row corruption. The support is that marker write happens on DB open, clean removal is gated on `close_db()`, direct `process.exit(1)` paths skip that cleanup, and packet 032 says recurrence after rebuild points toward abrupt kill. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1270`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:1649`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:105`. Confidence 0.78. Tag fullyNew.
+- CONFIRMED | The SQ1 `Connection closed` diagnostic is a symptom of launcher/client connection failure, not proof of DB corruption: the runner catches `client.connect()` failure and records `runner:mk-spec-memory`, and the captured evidence contains only that MCP connection-closed detail. Confidence 0.97. Tag partiallyNew.

+## Negative knowledge (directions ruled OUT this iteration, with why)
+
+- Ruled out "marker presence alone means the DB is corrupted." The marker is deliberately written on every DB open and removed only on clean close, so a live process or skipped cleanup can leave it present without proving FTS shadow corruption. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:739`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1270`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/vector-index-store.ts:1368`.
+- Ruled out `memory_index = memory_fts` row-count parity as a complete SQ2 repair/health proof. The known failure is in the FTS5 shadow and the repair decision says the malformed shadow branch was chosen after source-row duplicate IDs were ruled out. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:50`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/implementation-summary.md:79`.
+- Ruled out the boot integrity check as the mechanism that heals recurrence. The boot check logs `DETECT-ONLY` and explicitly attempts no DB recovery. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:371`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:373`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts:374`.
+- Ruled out a necessary one-to-one link between the SQ1 diagnostic row and `.unclean-shutdown`. With `SPECKIT_LAUNCHER_BRIDGE_DISABLED=1`, a live existing lease can make the launcher report and return before `launchServer()`, which can close the MCP client without opening the DB. Evidence: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/stress_test/substrate/run-substrate-stress-harness.mjs:619`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:322`, `.opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:327`, `.opencode/bin/mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:883`.
+- Ruled out treating packet 031/009 as end-to-end proof that live recurrence cannot happen. It verifies deterministic suites but still calls out the absence of a live two-launcher integration run. Evidence: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/031-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:55`, `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/031-embedding-stack-hardening/009-single-writer-durability-cluster/implementation-summary.md:111`.
+

The code proves how unclean markers recur and identifies direct cleanup-bypass exits, but this iteration did not prove which specific runtime event killed the daemon in the observed recurrence `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:11`.

SQ1 is still not evidence of a two-daemon double-write; the likely common cause is broader launcher/daemon lifecycle cleanup, and the exact overlap with SQ2 depends on whether the connection closed before or after DB initialization `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/032-infra-memory-db-and-graph-churn/research/deep-research-strategy.md:13`.
